sim_benchmark_contract.description: SRS-BT-005 (compare strategy performance against a user-selected benchmark defaulting to SPY; SyRS SYS-17 / SYS-36 / SYS-37; StRS SN-1.04). The benchmark selection, resolution-seam, and comparison-report surface lives in crates/atp-simulation (module `benchmark`) and WRAPS the SRS-BT-004 metric family: the SESSION 44 `metrics` slice already computes alpha/beta given a Benchmark (SPY default) and a BenchmarkPoint level series, so this slice adds the three pieces SRS-BT-005 names. (1) Selection: BenchmarkSelection resolves to SPY when the operator selects none (selection.resolve() applies self.selected.clone().unwrap_or_default(), the single place the SYS-17 SPY default is applied) and validates a user-selected ticker via Benchmark::new, mapping a malformed (empty / non-canonical) symbol to BenchmarkError::UnknownBenchmark so a report can never identify a benchmark it could not honestly name; is_default() lets a report state the comparison used the default. (2) Resolution: BenchmarkSource is the port that turns the resolved Benchmark into the integer-minor level series compute needs (symbol() plus levels(symbol, baseline_ts, axis) returning the pre-trade baseline level then one level per equity-curve mark in order, i.e. axis.len()+1 points). It mirrors the backtest::BarSource deferral seam: the real stored-data resolver is deferred (reads via the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 unified historical data interface (wiring it is SRS-BT-005's work)) and a fixture implementation drives the tests. The port is also an OPERATIONAL trust boundary: a real resolver's read can fail in ways that are not a malformed series (timeout, unavailable, not-found, or stale), so levels() surfaces a typed SourceFailure via BenchmarkError::SourceUnavailable, letting a caller retry / alert / fail closed rather than misclassifying an operational failure (stale-data blocking is one such outcome). (3) Comparison report: BenchmarkComparison is the data shape a dashboard/backtest report renders to IDENTIFY and contrast against its benchmark -- benchmark_symbol (the identity), is_default_benchmark, and the alpha, beta, strategy_total_return, benchmark_total_return, and excess_return ratios, each Option<f64> (None when undefined, never a fabricated zero). BenchmarkReport bundles the full PerformanceMetrics family with that comparison. compare() is BOUND TO THE STRATEGY RUN's evaluation window (a DateRange, e.g. BacktestResult.range), not a free baseline timestamp: it rejects an inverted window (InvalidWindow), requires every equity mark to fall within the window (EquityMarkOutsideWindow), and resolves the benchmark baseline AS-OF window.start (baseline_ts = window.start), which must open strictly before the first mark (WindowStartNotBeforeRun). This prevents a stale or unrelated baseline from measuring the benchmark over a different period than the strategy. (The full atomic run-snapshot identity -- a producer-stamped run id binding equity curve, trade log, and benchmark window into one snapshot -- is the deferred accumulator's responsibility, the same boundary as SRS-BT-004; this window binding is the in-scope coherence guard.) compare() then requires the source to serve the SAME symbol the report identifies (BenchmarkError::SourceSymbolMismatch), asks the source for the aligned series, and re-validates that series at the trust boundary BEFORE any metric is reported -- length == axis.len()+1 (SourceLengthMismatch), every level ts aligned to the baseline / mark (SourceTimestampMismatch), and every level strictly positive (NonPositiveSourceLevel) -- and only then calls metrics::compute, wrapping its MetricsError as BenchmarkError::Metrics (defense-in-depth re-validation). The numeric boundary is inherited from the metric family: levels and equity enter in integer minor units (level_minor: i64) and the comparison emits dimensionless f64 ratios (the metric domain, not a money leak); every emitted ratio is verified finite via fn finite_opt + is_finite() and fails closed (BenchmarkError::NonFiniteComparison) so a NaN/inf never reaches a ranking. The work is deterministic (SRS-BT-010): fixed left-to-right folds, no parallelism / RNG / wall-clock read. benchmark adds no broker/adapter dependency and carries no vendor SDK token; lib.rs re-exports `pub mod benchmark;`. The Rust integration test srs_bt_005_benchmark drives compare() end to end against a fixture source over a real BacktestEngine equity curve, including the foreign-window and source-unavailable degraded paths. The PASS line is 'SRS-BT-005 SDK-SURFACE PASS'. The operator benchmark-comparison RENDERING surface is now realized by the benchmark_comparison_cli binary (see benchmark_cli): it runs the SAME fixture strategy through the real BacktestEngine, resolves the benchmark through an in-binary fixture BenchmarkSource, runs the real compare(), and renders the identified comparison (SPY by default, a user-selected symbol via --benchmark) plus the eight metrics, with every undefined statistic shown as the literal `undefined` and every trust-boundary fault failing closed before any report line; the srs_bt_005_benchmark_cli integration test drives it in fresh processes. feature_list.json nevertheless KEEPS SRS-BT-005 passes:false: the acceptance criterion requires the WEB DASHBOARD AND backtest reports to identify the benchmark, and only the backtest-report identification leg (the CLI) is realized here. Two pieces remain genuinely deferred (named owners): the web dashboard / REST report rendering at the SYS-36 (<=5s) refresh / SYS-37 is the SRS-UI / SRS-API owner consuming BenchmarkComparison (the blocker keeping passes:false), and resolving the benchmark's ACTUAL historical level series from stored data is the SRS-BT-005 resolver-wiring behind BenchmarkSource (reading via the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 interface) (the CLI uses a fixture source).

benchmark_cli.note: The CLI half of the SRS-BT-005 operator benchmark-comparison rendering surface (the SYS-37 / SYS-36 web-dashboard + REST consumer of BenchmarkComparison remains the deferred SRS-UI / SRS-API owner). `defaults` prints DEFAULT_BENCHMARK_SYMBOL + DEFAULT_PERIODS_PER_YEAR and PROVES BenchmarkSelection::unselected() resolves to and identifies SPY (default-selection-resolves-to:SPY, is_default true) -- the 'if no benchmark is selected, SPY is used' half of the AC, made inspectable (a selection/identity-availability proof, since there is no single numeric SyRS constant to match). `run` runs the SAME fixture strategy once through the real BacktestEngine, resolves the benchmark through an in-binary fixture BenchmarkSource (the stand-in for the deferred (SRS-DATA-007 interface complete; real data = SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-FAC-001) stored-data resolver), runs the REAL compare(), and renders the resolved selection, the BenchmarkComparison (benchmark identity + alpha/beta + total/excess returns), and the eight SYS-16 metrics. `--benchmark SYM` is the user-selection seam (BenchmarkSelection::from_symbol): the report then identifies SYM (is_default false) instead of SPY, and alpha/beta are computed against it -- only the selection changes between runs, the strategy is fixed. The safety core (the SRS-BT-005 analog of the SRS-BT-002 'no cash fabricated' rule): every statistic the comparison leaves undefined (None) renders as the literal `undefined`, never a fabricated 0 or leaked NaN; and every --inject trust-boundary fault (a substituted symbol, a misaligned or length-mismatched series, a non-positive level, a typed operational source failure, or a foreign evaluation window) flows into compare(), which fails closed with a BenchmarkError BEFORE any report line prints (non-zero exit, no partial comparison -- a misaligned or substituted benchmark can never be reported as the selected one). The srs_bt_005_benchmark_cli integration test drives the binary in fresh processes. The real stored-data benchmark resolver (SRS-DATA-007) and the dashboard/REST rendering half (SRS-UI / SRS-API) remain deferred.

deferred.feature: Benchmark level-series resolution from stored data
deferred.what: compare() resolves a Benchmark and asks a BenchmarkSource for its integer-minor level series, but resolving SPY's (or a user-selected benchmark's) ACTUAL historical price levels from the data layer would read via SRS-DATA-007 (the unified historical data interface), which is now COMPLETE; wiring the real benchmark resolver to it (with the concrete-calendar bar grid for prior-close spacing, see SRS-FAC-001) is SRS-BT-005's remaining work. This slice ships the BenchmarkSource port (with its typed SourceFailure operational-failure contract) and a fixture implementation for tests (the benchmark_comparison_cli operator binary uses the same fixture-source stand-in); the real stored-data resolver (built on the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 interface) is not yet wired -- a fixture stands in -- so this real-data resolver stays deferred (one of the reasons SRS-BT-005 keeps passes:false).

compare() requires the benchmark baseline (levels[0]) to be strictly before the first equity mark (the pre-trade prior close), and binds the comparison to the run's inclusive window (equity marks within [start, end]). Verifying the baseline is the IMMEDIATE prior close -- not an arbitrarily stale earlier observation -- requires the data layer's actual bar grid (the spacing/calendar), which is the deferred (SRS-DATA-007 interface complete; real data = SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-FAC-001) resolver's responsibility; that resolver, having the bar grid, returns the correct prior close. The in-scope, deterministically-verifiable guard is that the baseline precedes the first mark and the equity curve falls within the window.

deferred.feature: Web dashboard / REST report benchmark identification
deferred.what: BenchmarkComparison is the data shape that identifies the benchmark (symbol, default flag, alpha/beta, total/excess return). The CLI rendering half is now REALIZED by the benchmark_comparison_cli operator binary (see benchmark_cli), which runs the real compare() over a fixture backtest and renders the identified comparison + metrics. Rendering it on the WEB DASHBOARD and in REST/WebSocket reports at the SYS-36 (<=5s) refresh / SYS-37 is the SRS-UI / SRS-API owner, which is not built; that dashboard/REST half remains deferred.

compare() binds the comparison to the strategy run's evaluation window (DateRange) and validates the equity curve falls within it, but the FULL run identity -- a producer-stamped run id binding the equity curve, trade log, and benchmark window into one consistent snapshot -- needs the deferred paper/live accumulator and an SRS-BT-001 run id, the same boundary deferred by SRS-BT-004. The window binding is the in-scope coherence guard.

deferred.feature: Persisting the benchmark comparison into the queryable backtest record
deferred.what: SRS-BT-009 (persist completed backtest results) names benchmark comparison as a queryable persisted field. Threading BenchmarkComparison into the persisted, queryable backtest record is the SRS-BT-009 owner (the natural next pick), not this slice.

sim_backtest_store_contract.description: SRS-BT-009 (persist completed backtest results; SyRS SYS-21 / SYS-79; StRS SN-1.02 / SN-1.04). The completed-backtest result persistence + query surface lives in crates/atp-simulation (module `backtest_store`) and WRAPS the producer types the prior slices already build -- it recomputes nothing. The acceptance criterion names seven artifacts that must be queryable by strategy, date range, and parameter set: BacktestRecord bundles all seven -- the BacktestRequest parameters (the parameter set), the SRS-BT-004 PerformanceMetrics family, the Vec<Fill> trade log, the Vec<EquityPoint> equity curve, the SRS-BT-005 BenchmarkComparison (the record's benchmark identity), an opaque caller-supplied CodeVersion (the strategy code version), and a producer-supplied completed_at_ts (the timestamp). RunId is the record's identity; BacktestRecord::new and BacktestResultStore::insert share one fail-closed validate_record (rejecting an empty id, an inverted run window, a negative starting cash, an invalid cost family, an equity mark outside the run window, a missing benchmark identity, or a non-finite ratio). BacktestResultStore answers the three SRS-BT-009 query axes -- query_by_strategy, query_by_run_window (the SYS-21 date-range axis -- records whose backtest run window request.range OVERLAPS the queried period, e.g. every 2020 backtest regardless of when it executed), query_by_completion_window (the distinct completion-timestamp axis), query_by_parameter_set (the StrategyParameters tuned set -- the named values that tell two points of a parameter sweep apart, distinct from the launch BacktestRequest), and a combined RecordQuery that ANDs them -- and holds records in a single canonical (completed_at_ts, run_id) order (a total order because run ids are unique, enforced by insert rejecting StoreError::DuplicateRunId), so every query returns deterministically and the serialized form is byte-identical for the same record set regardless of insertion order. serialize/restore is a deterministic, dependency-free text codec (MAGIC header + FNV-1a integrity checksum over the body, length-prefixed strings, sorted records, enum-tagged cost models): restore validates the magic, the checksum BEFORE building any state, the schema version, and every record invariant, decoding into a local Vec and sorting ONCE by order_key (O(n log n) bulk restore, not the O(n^2) of a per-record sorted insert) before constructing the store so a corrupt / truncated / checksum-mismatching blob yields no partially-restored store (StoreError::CorruptRecord, ChecksumMismatch, UnknownSchemaVersion). The numeric boundary: trade-log/equity money stays integer minor units (i128::from over the i64 *_minor fields) end to end, while the metric/comparison ratios are dimensionless f64 (the metric domain, inherited from SRS-BT-004/005, not a money leak) round-tripped EXACTLY via f64::to_bits / f64::from_bits and verified finite on restore (is_finite, StoreError::NonFiniteRatio) so a NaN/inf can never re-enter a ranking from a tampered blob. The work is deterministic (SRS-BT-010): fixed folds, no parallelism / RNG / wall-clock read; the completion timestamp is supplied by the producer. backtest_store adds no broker/adapter dependency and carries no vendor SDK token; lib.rs re-exports `pub mod backtest_store;`. The Rust integration test srs_bt_009_backtest_store drives a real BacktestEngine run -> metrics::compute -> benchmark::compare -> BacktestRecord -> store insert -> serialize -> restore -> query by all three axes end to end, including the corrupt-blob fail-closed and duplicate-run-id degraded paths. The FNV-1a integrity checksum is non-cryptographic: it detects ACCIDENTAL corruption (a change that does not also recompute it), NOT a deliberate checksum-recomputing tamperer (that needs a keyed MAC + key management, out of scope for the single-user, local-only baseline, the same stance as paper_state); the per-record invariant + domain checks are the independent defense that still rejects an impossible-valued recomputed blob. The durable FILE layer is shipped: save_to_path / load_from_path wrap the codec to atomically (temp+rename) write the whole store to STORE_FILENAME under a configured directory (the ATP_BACKTEST_RESULTS_DIR storage_paths key) and read it back, failing closed via Self::restore on a corrupt/checksum-mismatching file and restoring empty on a missing file (StoreError::Io for any other io failure). The PASS line is 'SRS-BT-009 SDK-SURFACE PASS'; feature_list.json keeps SRS-BT-009 passes:false because making that directory an SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERED store (eviction, NAS fallback) is the deferred SRS-DATA-008 owner, rendering the queryable history to an operator in the dashboard / backtest report is the deferred SRS-UI-004 / SRS-API owner, a full orchestrated run stamping a real run id / code version / completion timestamp end to end (the atomic run-snapshot identity) is the deferred SRS-BT-001 / orchestrator owner, and the operator persist/query workflow + the literal end-to-end step walk that flips the flag is Phase 2 -- the deterministic record + store + query + codec + durable-file surface is shipped here.

deferred.feature: SSD/NAS tiering of the persisted store
deferred.what: save_to_path / load_from_path now durably write the serialize/restore blob to a file under a caller-supplied directory (the ATP_BACKTEST_RESULTS_DIR storage_paths key) -- atomic temp+rename, fail-closed on a corrupt file, empty on a missing file -- so the in-scope 'persist' verb reaches disk. But making that directory an SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERED store (eviction, NAS fallback, tier promotion/demotion) is SRS-DATA-008, which is not built. The flag stays passes:false until Phase 2 walks the operator persist/query workflow end to end.

BacktestResultStore answers the three SRS-BT-009 query axes in-process, but rendering the queryable history TO AN OPERATOR (the backtest result history view, SYS-21) is the SRS-UI-004 / SRS-API owner consuming BacktestRecord, which is not built. The data + query contract is shipped here; the rendering surface is deferred.

BacktestRecord accepts a caller-supplied RunId, CodeVersion, and completed_at_ts and persists them, but a full orchestrated run that STAMPS a real run id, the strategy container image digest as the code version, and the completion timestamp end to end is SRS-BT-001 / the orchestrator. This slice persists provenance; it does not produce it.

A record binds its parameters, metrics, trade log, equity curve, and benchmark comparison together and validates the equity curve falls within the run window (the in-scope coherence guard), but the FULL run identity -- a producer-stamped run id atomically binding the equity curve, trade log, and benchmark window into one consistent snapshot -- needs the deferred paper/live accumulator + an SRS-BT-001 run id, the same boundary deferred by SRS-BT-004/005. Likewise, full VALUE-LEVEL metric verification (recomputing all eight metrics + alpha/beta from the stored curve/trade-log and confirming they match the stored values) needs the MetricsConfig (periods-per-year, risk-free rate) and the benchmark level series, neither of which SRS-BT-009 lists among its persisted fields; metric CORRECTNESS is the SRS-BT-004 producer's responsibility, and this layer enforces STRUCTURAL coherence (curve shape, trade-log ordering/window, benchmark identity, finiteness) -- the value-binding is this same deferred boundary.

file_persistence.description: SRS-BT-009 'persist' verb at the filesystem level: save_to_path / load_from_path WRAP the existing serialize/restore codec (no new format, no serde) to durably write the whole store to a file (STORE_FILENAME) under a caller-supplied directory. The write is CRASH-DURABLE and atomically published: it writes the blob to a per-call-UNIQUE scratch file (STORE_TMP_FILENAME base + a <pid>.<seq> suffix from std::process::id() + the SCRATCH_SEQ process-local counter, so two writers cannot rename over each other's scratch -- the suffix is NOT a clock/RNG read, so the persisted CONTENT stays byte-deterministic), fsyncs that scratch file (sync_all) so its bytes reach disk, fs::rename's it onto the live store (an atomic replace -- a reader never sees a half-written blob), and finally fsyncs the PARENT DIRECTORY (fs::File::open(dir) + sync_all) so the rename itself survives a crash. load_from_path has a fail-closed taxonomy: a MISSING configured DIRECTORY (!dir.is_dir(): unmounted / deleted / misconfigured storage path) fails closed as StoreError::Io rather than masquerading as an empty history (which would silently erase persisted runs); a present directory with NO store FILE (ErrorKind::NotFound) is the one legitimate empty-store / fresh-install case; a PRESENT file is delegated to Self::restore (so a corrupt / truncated / checksum-mismatching blob FAILS CLOSED -- a persisted run is never silently dropped); any other io error fails closed as StoreError::Io (a &'static str context label, because StoreError derives Clone/PartialEq/Eq which io::Error does not). The persisted directory is configured by the ATP_BACKTEST_RESULTS_DIR storage_paths key. Guarantee scope: this is a SINGLE-LOGICAL-WRITER durable store -- concurrent writers never corrupt the file (unique scratch + atomic rename) but the last publish wins; coordinating genuinely concurrent multi-writers (a lock / single-writer guard) and the SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERING, eviction, and failover of that directory remain deferred to SRS-DATA-008.

ingestion_idempotency_contract.description: SRS-DATA-016 (make ingestion jobs idempotent; SyRS NFR-R4; StRS SN-1.26 / SN-1.27). The acceptance: 'Re-running Databento, IB, option-chain, or Sharadar ingestion for an already ingested date creates no duplicate records and does not corrupt existing data.' This is the data-layer STORAGE SUBSTRATE -- the canonical market-data record, a local keyed store, and an idempotent validating write path -- the missing spine the rest of the SRS-DATA family composes (no canonical record / store / catalog existed before: atp-data shipped only the read-only ERR-5 validation + ERR-6 pacing gates, and the atp-adapters provider traits return record COUNTS, not records). It lives in crates/atp-data (module `store`). Every record carries a vendor-neutral NaturalKey = (DatasetKind, symbol, resolution, event_ts, option_contract) -- the dedup identity; the record's canonically-ordered MarketField value set (integer minor units) is its content. DatasetKind is the VENDOR-NEUTRAL taxonomy the four sources the acceptance names map onto (the core never names a vendor; the adapter layer maps provider -> kind, SRS-ARCH-003): DailyEquityBar <= Databento daily (SRS-DATA-001), MinuteEquityBar <= IB minute (SRS-DATA-002), OptionChainSnapshot <= IB option-chain (SRS-DATA-004), Fundamental <= Sharadar (SRS-DATA-005). MarketDataStore::upsert is the idempotency core: a fresh key INSERTS; an already-present key with IDENTICAL content is a silent no-op (UpsertOutcome::UnchangedDuplicate -- no duplicate row, the store stays byte-identical); an already-present key with DIFFERENT content fails closed (StoreError::ConflictingContent) leaving the existing record exactly as found (the 'corrupts existing data' guard). Records are held in one canonical natural-key order (binary_search_by), so the serialized form is byte-identical for the same record set regardless of ingest order -- which makes 'no duplicate / no corruption' inspectable on disk. DataLayer::ingest_market_record is the idempotent entry point: it COMPOSES the UNCHANGED ERR-5 validation gate (self.ingest_record -- so an invalid record is still quarantined read-only before any write) and only on a Valid classification applies the record via the idempotent store.upsert; the store mutator lives here / in store.rs, NEVER inside ingest_record (whose quarantine arm stays statically read-only per ingestion_validation_check.py forbidden_mutations). VALIDATION IS BOUND TO THE PERSISTED RECORD: the ERR-5 envelope is DERIVED from the record (record.ingestion_submission(): source = the dataset-kind tag, record_hash = the content fingerprint) rather than supplied independently, so the gate is always applied to exactly the record that will be written -- a caller cannot validate one payload and store another (there is no separate submission to forge). serialize/restore is a deterministic, dependency-free text codec (MAGIC header + FNV-1a integrity checksum over the body verified BEFORE building any state, length-prefixed strings, canonical-order records, untrusted counts grown incrementally not pre-allocated, single bulk sort on restore) that fails closed on a corrupt / truncated / checksum-mismatching / duplicate-key blob (StoreError::CorruptRecord / ChecksumMismatch / UnknownSchemaVersion / DuplicateKey) yielding no partial store. The durable FILE layer (save_to_path / load_from_path) mirrors the SRS-BT-009 backtest_store pattern: a per-call-unique scratch file, fsync, atomic fs::rename publish, parent-directory fsync; load fails closed on a missing/unmounted directory (!dir.is_dir() -> StoreError::Io, never a silent empty catalog) and restores an empty store only for a missing FILE in a provisioned directory. All value fields stay integer minor units (i64, i128 codec intermediates) -- no f64 in the core; the work is deterministic (no parallelism / RNG / wall-clock read); store carries no broker/adapter dependency and no lowercase vendor SDK token. The operator surface is the data016_ingest_cli binary (ingest / reingest / inspect over a fixture dir): reingest proves inserted:0 + bytes_identical:true across all four kinds. The Rust integration test srs_data_016_idempotent_ingest drives ingest -> persist -> reload -> re-ingest -> assert no duplicate + byte-identical file + conflicting-content fail-closed + quarantined-record-not-written, per kind. SRS-DATA-016 flips passes:true: the idempotency property is a self-contained runtime property (verification: Test) demonstrated end to end with fixture sources + persisted-file inspection, exactly as the step permits. DEFERRED (named owners, none load-bearing for idempotency): the REAL Databento/IB/Sharadar/option-chain network adapters (SRS-DATA-001/003/005/006, the atp-adapters provider traits stay stubs); unified query read consumers -- strategy code, notebooks (SRS-DATA-007); SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERING, eviction, cold-read failover of the store directory (SRS-DATA-008/009/010); the validator rule logic + quarantine dashboard/notification alert surface (SRS-DATA-013 / SRS-NOTIF-001).

record_hash.description: The IngestionRecordSubmission type contract (atp-types) specifies record_hash as the canonical SHA-256 of the normalized record bytes. MarketDataRecord::ingestion_submission derives record_hash as sha256::hex over normalized_bytes() -- the canonical full-record encoding (encode_record: kind + symbol + resolution + event_ts + option_contract + the value fields), so the hash covers the WHOLE record (key INCLUDED) and two distinct records can never share a record_hash (no value-only collision across keys). SHA-256 is a dependency-free safe-u32 impl (the same zero-dependency discipline as the FNV-1a checksum) pinned by the FIPS 180-4 known-answer vectors.

store_lock.description: SRS-DATA-016 no-corruption under CONCURRENT ingestion jobs. A single save_to_path is atomic, but a load-modify-save done by two writers concurrently would be last-publish-wins -- the later save could erase the earlier writer's newly ingested records. StoreLock is the single-writer guard held across the WHOLE load-modify-save (the operator CLI's ingest/reingest acquire it): acquisition is an atomic exclusive file create (OpenOptions::create_new, i.e. O_EXCL) of LOCK_FILENAME, so a second concurrent writer is REFUSED (StoreError::Locked) rather than proceeding to a silent overwrite; the lock requires an existing directory (a missing dir fails closed as StoreError::Io, symmetric with load_from_path) and is released on Drop (the file is removed). Scope: serializes writers on one host/filesystem (the single-user/local baseline; the orchestrator serializes scheduled ingestion jobs). A crashed holder leaves a stale lock the operator removes before retrying; pid-liveness/lease expiry and cross-host coordination are out of scope.

reingest_proof.description: The operator `reingest` command is a PURE, NON-MUTATING idempotency proof: it re-ingests into an in-memory copy of the loaded store and verifies every outcome was a no-op (inserted == 0, length unchanged) and that the post-reingest store re-serializes to exactly the on-disk bytes, but it NEVER calls save_to_path. So running reingest on the wrong kind/date or a fresh directory fails closed WITHOUT persisting the newly-inserted records -- a failed proof can never become a state-changing ingest.

deferred: Real Databento / IB / Sharadar / option-chain NETWORK adapters that materialize records from the live providers (owner: SRS-DATA-001 / SRS-DATA-003 / SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-DATA-006; the atp-adapters provider traits stay not_configured stubs -- fixture sources stand in, as the SRS-DATA-016 step permits)

Unified historical query READ consumers -- strategy code, backtests, factor jobs, notebooks (read via the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 interface; this idempotent store's records()/get() is the substrate they consume -- no longer a DATA-016 deferral)
SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERING, eviction, and cold-read failover of the store directory (owner: SRS-DATA-008 / SRS-DATA-009 / SRS-DATA-010)

The concrete RecordValidator SYS-77 rule logic + the quarantine dashboard/notification alert surface (owner: SRS-DATA-013 / SRS-DATA-014 / SRS-NOTIF-001; ingest_market_record composes the UNCHANGED ERR-5 gate)

fundamental_ingestion_contract.description: SRS-DATA-005 (ingest Sharadar fundamental data on a scheduled basis; SyRS SYS-26, NFR-P8d; StRS SN-3.03 / BG-3). The acceptance: 'Income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement, and key ratio records for US equities are ingested, validated, cataloged, available to the factor pipeline, and completed within the overnight window of 16:00 ET to 09:30 ET next trading day.' This block pins the fundamental-ingestion SUBSTRATE over the SRS-DATA-016 storage spine. The dependency direction (SRS-ARCH-002) forces a three-crate decomposition meeting at ONE shared boundary type, because atp-adapters may depend ONLY on atp-types and atp-data may depend ONLY on atp-types (neither may depend on the other): (a) atp-types defines FundamentalStatements -- a vendor-neutral bundle of one fiscal period's statements for one US equity, all integer minor units, with period_end_ts (the fiscal PERIOD END, the natural-key event_ts) SEPARATE from available_ts (the FILING / availability instant). Its constructor fails closed on an empty symbol, a negative period end, available_ts < period_end_ts (impossible provenance -- a statement cannot be filed before its period ends), or a non-positive market_value_minor (the key-ratio denominator the factor pipeline divides by). net_income_minor / book_equity_minor MAY be negative (a loss-making or negative-book-value security). (b) atp-data (module `fundamentals`) build_fundamental_records turns a validated FundamentalStatements into the FOUR canonical Fundamental store records -- one per statement type, all sharing the natural key (DatasetKind::Fundamental, symbol, event_ts = period_end_ts) and differing only by resolution: fundamental:income, fundamental:balance, fundamental:cashflow, fundamental:ratios. Each carries available_ts so a point-in-time consumer of ANY statement gates on availability. The ratios record carries EXACTLY the four fields the factor loader reads -- available_ts, net_income_minor, book_equity_minor, market_value_minor -- so a built bundle is always readable by atp_factor_pipeline::store_inputs::load_fundamental_input (resolution fundamental:ratios; the resolution literal is duplicated in atp-data because the core data layer must NOT depend on the factor-pipeline crate, and the SRS-DATA-005 integration test reads a built record back THROUGH the real loader so any drift fails closed). (c) atp-adapters SharadarAdapter::map_fundamentals maps Sharadar SF1 rows (the real vendor columns ticker / reportperiod = period end / datekey = filing instant, plus the minor-unit line items netinc / equity / marketcap / revenue / assets / liabilities / ncfo / ncfi / ncff) onto FundamentalStatements -- the load-bearing provider -> vendor-neutral mapping (SRS-ARCH-003: the adapter layer maps provider -> kind so the core never names a vendor). A malformed vendor row (filed-before-period-end, non-positive market cap, empty ticker) fails closed via the DTO constructor. The records flow through the UNCHANGED DataLayer::ingest_market_record path (the ERR-5 validation gate + the idempotent store.upsert) -- so 'ingested, validated, cataloged' reuses the SRS-DATA-016 substrate and re-running an already-ingested period is a no-op (no duplicate, byte-identical). The operator surface is the data005_fundamental_cli binary (ingest / reingest / inspect / factor-input over a fixture dir): reingest proves inserted:0 + bytes_identical:true; factor-input reads the point-in-time fundamental:ratios record back by symbol / resolution with NO provider named (the SRS-DATA-007 read surface) and prints the derived earnings_yield / book_to_price. The Rust integration test srs_data_005_fundamental_ingest (in atp-factor-pipeline, the one crate allowed to depend on BOTH atp-data and the loader) drives build -> ingest -> persist -> reload -> re-ingest (idempotent) -> READ via the real load_fundamental_input, plus the point-in-time skip (a not-yet-filed statement is an auditable absence) and the negatives-allowed path. SRS-DATA-005 STAYS passes:false (foundational substrate): the vendor-neutral mapping + the four-statement record build + the validation / idempotent-catalog write + the loader-availability read are demonstrated end to end over fixture sources, but the close needs the deferred owners below. All value fields stay integer minor units (no f64 in the core); the work is deterministic; atp-data fundamentals and atp-types carry NO lowercase vendor SDK token (the Sharadar token lives ONLY in atp-adapters, SRS-ARCH-003). DEFERRED (named owners, none load-bearing for the substrate): the REAL Sharadar network adapter (live API auth + fetch -- the atp-adapters FundamentalDataProvider::ingest_fundamentals stays a not_configured stub, fixture rows stand in); the NFR-P8d overnight-window (16:00->09:30 ET) wall-clock completion proof over the full 8,000+ US-equity universe against a real clock (a wall-clock performance harness, deferred exactly like SRS-FAC-001's NFR-P7 -- the fixture run uses a deterministic window, proving the mapping/validation/catalog/availability LOGIC, not wall-clock completion); the in-process orchestrated fetch->ingest HOST that wires the adapter to the data layer (the orchestrator -- no crate depends on both adapter and data); the concrete RecordValidator SYS-77 fundamental field-range rules + quarantine alert surface (SRS-DATA-013 / SRS-NOTIF-001).

loader_contract.description: The factor pipeline's load_fundamental_input reads the fundamental:ratios record; the builder's ratios record must carry exactly these fields and resolution or the integration test fails closed.

dimension_policy.description: Sharadar SF1 emits multiple rows per (ticker, reportperiod) distinguished by a dimension column (ARQ/MRQ/ARY/MRY/ART/MRT). The substrate ingests EXACTLY the as-reported quarterly dimension (ARQ -- point-in-time honest, no restatement back-fill / lookahead) and REJECTS every other dimension fail-closed, so two rows for the same ticker+period that differ only by dimension can never collapse onto one vendor-neutral identity. Multi-dimension support needs the same filing-version store key as restatements (a deferred storage-schema change).

unified_query_runtime_contract.description: SRS-DATA-007 (provide a unified historical data access interface; SyRS SYS-27 / SYS-53; StRS SN-1.28 / SN-3.03 / BG-5). The acceptance: 'Strategy code, backtests, factor jobs, and notebooks query by symbol, date range, and resolution WITHOUT specifying the original source provider.' This is the runnable READ path over the SRS-DATA-016 storage substrate (the canonical MarketDataStore of vendor-neutral records the data layer already persists) -- distinct from the unified_historical_data (API-7) block, which pins the provider-facing adapter TRAIT shape (HistoricalDataAdapter in atp-adapters); this block pins the runtime query that actually serves records from the store. It lives in crates/atp-data (module `query`). UnifiedHistoricalQuery carries ONLY the three acceptance dimensions -- symbol, resolution, and an inclusive [start_ts, end_ts] event-timestamp range -- plus an optional DatasetKind disambiguator; there is deliberately NO provider/vendor/source/feed parameter (a consumer literally cannot pass one), and DatasetKind is the VENDOR-NEUTRAL taxonomy (daily bar / minute bar / option-chain / fundamental), never a provider name. UnifiedHistoricalResult echoes the queried symbol + resolution and the matched records, with NO provider/vendor/source field -- the envelope cannot name a record's origin, so a strategy / backtest / factor job / notebook is structurally unable to branch on it. MarketDataStore::query_unified filters self.records() (the canonical natural-key order the store already maintains) on exactly those vendor-neutral NaturalKey dimensions -- symbol equality, resolution equality, the inclusive event_ts range, and the optional kind -- and returns the survivors sorted explicitly into deterministic event_ts-ascending order with the full natural key as a total-order tiebreaker (the store's canonical order sorts by kind BEFORE event_ts, so a kind-agnostic match spanning two kinds that share symbol+resolution needs the explicit event_ts sort to honor the date-range contract), no clock, no RNG; an empty match is a RETURNED value, never an error. For a kind-NARROWED query (the common case -- every per-series consumer names a kind) the records are read by BINARY SEARCH over the canonical order (MarketDataStore::records_for finds the contiguous (kind, symbol, resolution) block, then the inclusive event_ts sub-range), O(log n + matches) and the SAME records in the SAME order as the full scan -- so a full-universe assembly that reads once per security is NOT O(universe * store_size). The SAME one method serves every provider kind the acceptance names -- DailyEquityBar (<= Databento), MinuteEquityBar (<= IB), OptionChainSnapshot (<= IB option-chain), Fundamental (<= Sharadar) -- so a multi-provider catalog has exactly one read path and no provider-specific branch. The operator surface is the data007_query_cli binary (query --dir --symbol --resolution --start --end [--kind]): it loads the atomically-published on-disk snapshot read-only (NO single-writer StoreLock -- a read does not need it; concurrent-read-DURING-write coordination is the deferred SRS-DATA-017) and prints a source-neutral key:value report (symbol / resolution / start / end / kind / match_count, then each record's event_ts + option_contract + integer-minor value fields) with no provider/source/vendor line. The Rust integration test srs_data_007_unified_query drives ingest (fixtures, all four kinds) -> persist -> reload -> query and asserts symbol/resolution/range filtering, cross-provider-kind uniformity, the optional-kind narrowing, determinism across a persisted reload, and empty-is-not-an-error. SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE and closes to passes:true at integration (close_feature.py --verified flips feature_list.json under the scheduler lock; this branch deliberately does NOT edit that file) -- its AC is a CONTRACT TEST (docs/SRS.md line 177) proving all four named consumers query this provider-neutral interface by symbol/date-range/resolution: the source-neutral query ENGINE + operator CLI + contract test are demonstrated end to end over fixture data + persisted-output inspection, AND the in-process Python consumer binding (StoreBackedHistoricalData; store_history_binding_contract) reads this engine by symbol / date range / resolution with no provider named via the explicit RAW path. The shared HistoricalData Protocol is get_bars (the lookback idiom -- itself a bounded date-window query); the binding offers a binding-specific get_bars_range for an explicit [start, end] window, and the unified engine, the backtest StoreBarSource, and the factor store_inputs loaders all take an explicit [start, end] range -- so DATA-007's date-range AC is met on every consumer. Adding an explicit-range method to the public runtime_checkable Protocol (so a Protocol-TYPED reference, not only the concrete binding, exposes it) is an SRS-SDK-001 strategy-authoring-surface enhancement, NOT a DATA-007 requirement. All four named consumers query this interface provider-neutrally: (1) the binding serves RAW verbatim AND the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED Protocol default through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage gate (an uncovered query fails closed with CoverageNotProvenError, never raw-as-adjusted), so the prior raw-as-adjusted hazard is resolved; (2) the BACKTEST consumer is now genuinely wired (atp_simulation::store_bar_source::StoreBarSource consumes this store path in BacktestEngine::run) and strategy + notebook / research code read via the binding (StoreBackedHistoricalData.get_bars_range); (3) the FACTOR-JOB consumer now READS the store -- atp_factor_pipeline::store_inputs (load_daily_market_input + load_fundamental_input + assemble_factor_inputs) sources BOTH the market and fundamental inputs by symbol / date range / resolution with no provider named (point-in-time SAFE loaders), and run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store composes that read with the schedule gate, DERIVING the data as-of from the calendar (TradingCalendar::session_as_of_ts) for the scheduled session -- so a caller cannot pair a session with a future as-of; the concrete real-calendar SessionOrdinal<->epoch mapping is the deferred owner (see SRS-FAC-001), not part of the DATA-007 read surface. The Jupyter notebook HOST runtime is the SEPARATELY-specified SRS-RES-002 feature (docs/SRS.md line 209, verification 'Test, demonstration' -- kernel / plotting / no-live-order isolation), DISTINCT from this DATA-007 interface (line 177, verification 'Contract test'); the notebook DATA ACCESS goes through the same binding all consumers use, so DATA-007's contract-test AC is met independent of the RES-002 host. Earlier passes also fixed a kind-agnostic event_ts-ordering bug and made split adjustment fail closed for non-equity kinds. SRS-DATA-012 ALSO stays passes:false (live-subscription + FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN dividend modes deferred). concurrent_read_runtime_contract.description: SRS-DATA-017 (support concurrent reads during ingestion writes; SyRS SYS-63; StRS SN-1.26 / SN-1.28). Acceptance: 'Strategy containers, backtests, factor jobs, and notebooks read previously ingested data while ingestion jobs write new data without corruption or blocking completed data.' Verification mode: Load test. This is a runtime CONCURRENCY property of the SRS-DATA-016 storage substrate (MarketDataStore in crates/atp-data, module `store`), not a new code surface -- the substrate already provides snapshot-isolated reads. (1) A WRITER (an ingestion job, e.g. data016_ingest_cli ingest) serializes behind the exclusive single-writer StoreLock (acquire = O_EXCL create_new(true); a second concurrent writer is refused with StoreError::Locked, never a last-publish-wins overwrite) and persists via save_to_path, which writes a per-call-unique scratch file, fsyncs it (sync_all), then atomically replaces the live store with fs::rename and fsyncs the parent directory -- so a concurrent reader observes either the whole old file or the whole new file, never a torn read. (2) A READER (a strategy container / backtest / factor job / notebook -- here the lock-free data007_query_cli query and data016_ingest_cli inspect operator surfaces, which exercise the SAME MarketDataStore::load_from_path path those in-process consumers use) acquires NO lock, so a read never blocks on, nor is blocked by, an active ingestion write; each load_from_path is a consistent point-in-time snapshot of the atomically-published file. (3) Reads FAIL CLOSED on corruption: load_from_path routes a present file through the checksum-first restore (the FNV-1a integrity checksum over the body is verified -- checksum(body) != stored_checksum -> StoreError::ChecksumMismatch -- BEFORE any record is decoded), so a partial / corrupt read is an error, never a silently-partial store. Together these are snapshot isolation via copy-on-write atomic file replacement + a single-writer lock + lock-free readers (the classic 'readers never block writers, writers never block readers, no torn reads' guarantee). The Load test (the AC's named verification mode) is realized two ways: the always-run Rust integration test srs_data_017_concurrent_reads drives a lock-held writer thread doing many load-modify-save ingests of distinct keys concurrently with several lock-free reader threads, asserting every read is Ok (never ChecksumMismatch / CorruptRecord), every read still contains the full previously-ingested seed set (completed data is never lost or blocked), the observed record count is monotonic non-decreasing, and no reader ever observes StoreError::Locked; and the gated (ATP_RUN_INTEGRATION=1) cross-PROCESS Python load test runs the real data016_ingest_cli writer process concurrently with data007_query_cli + inspect reader processes (separate OS processes -- the faithful strategy-container analog) and asserts every concurrent read succeeds with the seed present and no corruption. SRS-DATA-017 STAYS passes:false (foundational substrate, NOT a feature close): the substrate concurrency guarantee is demonstrated end to end by a Load test exercising the exact lock-free read path the named consumers use, but the acceptance NAMES strategy containers / backtests / factor jobs / notebooks as the readers, and while the FIRST in-process Python consumer binding now EXISTS (StoreBackedHistoricalData, store_history_binding_contract, which reads via the lock-free path), the concurrent-read-DURING-write Load test for THAT named Python consumer -- a Python-consumer-vs-held-writer load test, not the Rust-CLI analog -- is not yet in place; so flipping passes:true would over-claim the AC's named-consumer concurrency end to end. The Python-consumer-vs-held-writer Load test is the LOAD-BEARING deferral for the feature close; this block is the substrate evidence + a regression gate. DEFERRED (named owners): the concurrent-read-DURING-write Load test for the now-wired Python consumer binding (StoreBackedHistoricalData / store_history_binding_contract) and the dashboard / REST consumer surfaces (SRS-UI / SRS-API); the real Databento / IB / Sharadar / option-chain NETWORK adapters that materialize records (SRS-DATA-001 / SRS-DATA-003 / SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-DATA-006; fixture sources stand in); SSD-primary / NAS-archival tiering + eviction + cold-read failover of the store directory (SRS-DATA-008 / SRS-DATA-009 / SRS-DATA-010); richer lock liveness (pid-liveness / lease expiry) and cross-host coordination (the single-user, local-one-host baseline serializes writers on one filesystem).
deferred: The concurrent-read-DURING-write Load test for the now-wired Python consumer binding (StoreBackedHistoricalData / store_history_binding_contract reads via the lock-free path; a Python-consumer-vs-held-writer Load test is the remaining 017 close) plus the dashboard / REST surfaces (owner: SRS-UI / SRS-API)

Real Databento / IB / Sharadar / option-chain NETWORK adapters that materialize records (owner: SRS-DATA-001 / SRS-DATA-003 / SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-DATA-006; fixture sources stand in, as the verification step permits)
SSD-primary / NAS-archival TIERING, eviction, and cold-read failover of the store directory (owner: SRS-DATA-008 / SRS-DATA-009 / SRS-DATA-010)
Richer lock liveness (pid-liveness / lease expiry) and cross-host write coordination -- the single-user, local-one-host baseline serializes writers on one filesystem (StoreLock scope, documented)

store_history_binding_contract.description: SRS-DATA-007 FOUNDATIONAL CONSUMER BINDING -- the FIRST in-process Python consumer binding over the unified historical query engine (SyRS SYS-27 / SYS-53; StRS SN-1.28 / SN-3.03 / BG-5). The unified_query_runtime_contract pins the Rust substrate (the source-neutral MarketDataStore::query_unified engine + the data007_query_cli operator surface); this block pins the Python StoreBackedHistoricalData (python/atp_strategy/store_history.py), a concrete atp_strategy.api.HistoricalData implementation that is the load-bearing groundwork toward the close. The acceptance NAMES strategy code / backtests / factor jobs / notebooks as the consumers, and all four consume the one HistoricalData Protocol -- so a single concrete, store-backed implementation gives all of them provider-neutral access via the Protocol's get_bars(symbol, lookback, frequency, end) -- symbol + a lookback-idiom date range (the upper bound end + the lookback count of bars) + resolution, with NO provider named. The concrete binding ADDITIONALLY offers a binding-specific deterministic get_bars_range(symbol, frequency, start, end) helper for backtests / factor jobs that want an explicit reproducible [start, end] range; get_bars_range is NOT part of the public HistoricalData Protocol (which is get_bars), so consumers typed to the Protocol get the lookback-idiom range only. Adding an explicit-[start, end] range method to the SHARED runtime_checkable Protocol is an SRS-SDK-001 strategy-authoring-surface enhancement (the Protocol is the strategy API's interface; promoting the method would require every implementer -- the warm-up / parity / harness stubs -- to add it), NOT a DATA-007 requirement: DATA-007's date-range AC is already met -- get_bars(lookback, end) is a bounded date-window query, and the unified engine, the backtest StoreBarSource, the factor store_inputs loaders, and this binding's get_bars_range all take an explicit [start, end] range. The binding drives the lock-free, source-neutral data007_query_cli over a subprocess (the repo's only cross-language boundary pattern; there is no PyO3/maturin toolchain in scope) and parses its source-neutral key:value stdout into Bar objects. Source-neutral by construction: there is NO provider/vendor/source/feed parameter on the query methods (a consumer cannot specify an origin) and no origin field is read off the result. An equity query IS narrowed to the vendor-neutral equity-bar DatasetKind via the --kind disambiguator (a dataset TYPE, not a provider) so a fundamental / option-chain record that happens to share the same symbol + resolution cannot poison the OHLCV-bar read; only the daily ('1d') and minute ('1m') equity-bar datasets are in scope (richer resolutions need SRS-SDK-007 bar consolidation, deferred -- an unsupported resolution fails closed). Honesty boundaries an adversarial reviewer checks: (1) normalization -- the binding serves NormalizationMode.RAW (verbatim) and the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED (the HistoricalData Protocol default): split-adjusted is routed through the data007_query_cli SRS-DATA-011 coverage gate (MarketDataStore::query_split_adjusted), and the binding validates the echoed coverage_through frontier (gate-integrity: present iff split-adjusted, >= end) and raises CoverageNotProvenError (a StoreQueryError, naming SRS-DATA-011) when the symbol is not covered through the query end, so a caller never gets raw bars dressed up as adjusted. FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN stay OUT of the served map and FAIL CLOSED with NotImplementedError (dividend data, SRS-DATA-012). The raw split-adjustment math is crate-internal (see normalization_modes_contract); the coverage gate is the only path to it; (2) money units -- stored OHLC values are integer minor units, scaled to major units by the single named _PRICE_MINOR_SCALE (= 100, the cents convention the fixtures use: close = seed*100), while volume is a raw integer count that is NEVER divided by the scale; the authoritative SDK<->core money-unit boundary is deferred (atp-types order_type.rs) and _PRICE_MINOR_SCALE is the one place to change when it is pinned; (3) lock-free read -- the binding takes no StoreLock (the data007_query_cli read path takes none), so it exercises the SRS-DATA-017 read-during-write property's read side; (4) subprocess safety -- argv is a list (shell=False), every CLI call is bounded by a per-query timeout (a wedged CLI raises subprocess.TimeoutExpired which is mapped to StoreQueryError, never an indefinite hang of a strategy container), match_count:0 is a returned [] (empty is a value, never an error), and a non-zero exit / missing binary / missing OHLCV field fails closed (a Bar is never fabricated); the parser also requires the parsed record indexes to cover EXACTLY [0, match_count), the CLI-echoed symbol/resolution/start/end to MATCH the request, every record's event_ts to fall inside the requested inclusive [start, end] range, and the records to be event_ts-ascending before building any Bar, so a truncated / drifted / mislabelled / out-of-range / misordered response (a wrong or stale query binary) fails closed rather than feeding partial, relabelled, future/stale, or misordered history to a strategy / backtest / factor job. Verification: the L4 boundary test exercises parsing over a fake runner; the L5 integration test (ATP_RUN_INTEGRATION=1) ingests fixtures via data016_ingest_cli then reads them back through the Python binding; the L7 domain test wires a real Strategy subclass (standing in for strategy / backtest / factor-job / notebook code) that pulls its own history via the binding over a real ingested store and asserts it receives store-sourced bars with no provider, plus checks that over a COVERED store (bar + 4-for-1 split + asserted coverage frontier) the bare-default (SPLIT_ADJUSTED) query returns the re-quoted adjusted series (close 2500, volume 400000) and over an UNCOVERED store it FAILS CLOSED with CoverageNotProvenError (naming SRS-DATA-011), never raw-as-adjusted; the L3 contract test + tools/store_history_check.py pin the source-neutral / normalization-honesty / money-scale / list-argv invariants with per-guard non-vacuity mutations and a cargo round-trip. SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE and closes to passes:true at integration (close_feature.py --verified flips feature_list.json under the scheduler lock; this branch deliberately does NOT edit that file); this binding is one of its four wired consumers: a real in-process consumer reads ingested data by symbol/date-range/resolution with no provider, serving RAW verbatim and the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED Protocol default through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage gate (the L7 demonstration covers both the covered->adjusted and the uncovered->CoverageNotProvenError paths -- a strategy can never silently consume raw-as-adjusted bars). All four named consumers now read via this store path: backtests through atp-simulation StoreBarSource (BacktestEngine::run), factor jobs through atp-factor-pipeline store_inputs + run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store, and strategy + notebook code through this binding -- a notebook querying the interface is plain Python importing this binding, so the notebook DATA ACCESS is wired (the Jupyter HOST runtime that RUNS notebooks is the SEPARATE SRS-RES-002 feature, docs/SRS.md line 209). FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN need dividend data (SRS-DATA-012). DEFERRED (named owners): normalization-mode application (SRS-DATA-012); the concurrent-read-DURING-write Load test for THIS named Python consumer (SRS-DATA-017; the substrate guarantee is proven and the binding uses the lock-free path, but the Python-consumer-vs-held-writer Load test is the 017 close); real Databento/IB/Sharadar/option-chain network adapters (SRS-DATA-001/003/005/006; fixtures stand in); option-chain bar access (the binding serves EQUITY OHLCV bars; OPTION raises) and the dashboard/REST consumer surfaces (SRS-UI / SRS-API); an authoritative SDK<->core money-unit scale constant.
deferred: The Jupyter notebook HOST runtime. The BACKTEST consumer is now genuinely wired -- atp_simulation::store_bar_source::StoreBarSource is a real BarSource the BacktestEngine consumes over the unified MarketDataStore (raw + gated split-adjusted); the FACTOR-JOB consumer now READS the store -- atp_factor_pipeline::store_inputs (load_daily_market_input + load_fundamental_input + assemble_factor_inputs) sources BOTH the market and fundamental inputs as point-in-time read primitives, and run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store composes that read with the schedule gate, DERIVING the data as-of from the calendar's session_as_of_ts for the scheduled session (so a caller cannot pair a session with a future as-of; only the concrete real-calendar mapping is deferred, see SRS-FAC-001) -- and strategy + notebook / research code read via the binding (ctx.history / StoreBackedHistoricalData.get_bars_range). The Jupyter notebook HOST runtime (kernel / plotting / no-live-order isolation) is the SEPARATE SRS-RES-002 feature, not a DATA-007 consumer gap -- DATA-007 already serves the notebook DATA ACCESS through this binding (owner: SRS-RES-002 for the host runtime)

FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN normalization + the live-subscription normalization path (owner: SRS-DATA-012); split-adjusted is now SERVED by the binding through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage gate (RAW verbatim + gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED, the Protocol default; uncovered fails closed with CoverageNotProvenError, never raw-as-adjusted)

An explicit-[start, end] range method ON THE PUBLIC runtime_checkable HistoricalData Protocol (owner: SRS-SDK-001, the strategy-authoring API surface) -- NOT a DATA-007 gap: DATA-007's date-range AC is met (the Protocol's get_bars(lookback, end) is a bounded date-window query, and the unified engine / backtest StoreBarSource / factor store_inputs / this binding's get_bars_range all take an explicit [start, end] range); exposing the method on the runtime_checkable Protocol itself, so a Protocol-TYPED reference -- not only the concrete binding -- gets it, is the SRS-SDK-001 authoring-surface concern

The concurrent-read-DURING-write Load test for THIS named Python consumer (owner: SRS-DATA-017; the binding drives the lock-free read path and the substrate guarantee is proven, but the Python-consumer-vs-held-writer Load test is the deferred 017 close)
Real Databento / IB / Sharadar / option-chain NETWORK adapters that materialize records (owner: SRS-DATA-001 / SRS-DATA-003 / SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-DATA-006; fixture sources stand in)
An authoritative SDK<->core money-unit scale constant -- the binding assumes the cents (x100) fixture convention for equity OHLC (owner: deferred runtime money boundary, atp-types)
Option-chain bar access (the binding serves EQUITY OHLCV bars; OPTION raises) and the dashboard / REST consumer surfaces (owner: SRS-UI / SRS-API)

normalization_modes_contract.description: SRS-DATA-012 SPLIT-ADJUSTED HISTORICAL NORMALIZATION (SyRS SYS-29 / StRS SN-1.15). Acceptance: "support raw, split-adjusted, fully adjusted, and total-return normalization modes per security subscription; historical and live subscription requests can select a normalization mode; options strategies can request raw prices; indicators can request adjusted series." This ships the HISTORICAL split-adjusted slice as FOUNDATIONAL substrate -- the Rust core split-adjustment math, now served on the operator CLI ONLY through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage-enforcing gate (data007_query_cli --normalization split-adjusted routes through MarketDataStore::query_split_adjusted, which fails closed when the symbol is not covered through --end). It does NOT close SRS-DATA-012 (which STAYS passes:false): SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE -- the binding serves the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED Protocol default (the prior raw-as-adjusted hazard is resolved -- an uncovered read fails closed with CoverageNotProvenError naming SRS-DATA-011), and the named backtest/factor/notebook consumers now read via this path; SRS-DATA-012 is the block that STAYS passes:false here -- its LIVE subscription normalization path and the FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN dividend modes remain deferred. Without proven coverage a split-adjusted read over a store with no split facts would be raw-as-adjusted (the Codex adversarial pass flagged this fail-open), so the gate fails closed without coverage on both the operator CLI and the consumer binding. The math lives in the Rust core (crates/atp-data/src/normalization.rs, the single source of truth): for a bar at event_ts t, over every split with effective_ts > t (strict -- a bar ON the effective date is already post-split), NUM = product of numerators and DEN = product of denominators; OHLC = round(raw * DEN / NUM), volume = round(raw * NUM / DEN). Three disciplines an adversarial reviewer checks: compose-then-divide (all numerators/denominators multiplied first, exactly ONE division per field -- no compounding rounding across multiple splits); i128 intermediates with a fail-closed i64::try_from narrowing (money never silently wraps) and rejection of a non-positive split factor; round-half-to-even (banker's) so a long backtest has zero rounding bias (truncation would drift prices down). Split corporate actions persist as a vendor-neutral DatasetKind::CorporateActionSplit in the SAME hardened idempotent/durable/deterministic MarketDataStore as bars (keyed (symbol, effective_ts) with numerator/denominator fields), so the adjustment reads splits from the same atomic snapshot the bars came from. data007_query_cli serves --normalization raw, and split-adjusted ONLY through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage-enforcing gate (MarketDataStore::query_split_adjusted): split-adjusted requires an equity-bar --kind and FAILS closed (naming SRS-DATA-011) when the symbol is not covered through --end, so the CLI never emits raw-as-adjusted output; fully-adjusted / total-return still fail closed (dividend data deferred). The Python StoreBackedHistoricalData consumer binding serves RAW verbatim and the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED Protocol default through the same coverage gate: an uncovered split-adjusted read fails closed with CoverageNotProvenError (naming SRS-DATA-011), never raw-as-adjusted, and the binding validates the echoed coverage_through frontier (gate-integrity); FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN still fail closed (dividend data). the named backtest / factor / notebook consumers now read via this path (SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE). The RAW split math (split_adjust_records / SplitEvent) stays available ONLY behind the coverage gate: the split-adjustment module is CRATE-INTERNAL (lib.rs declares `mod normalization`, private, and re-exports NONE of split_adjust_records / SplitEvent), so a Rust consumer cannot call it directly and obtain split-adjusted IDENTITY values over an empty/incomplete split set (raw-as-adjusted) -- the only public path to split-adjusted output is the coverage gate (query_split_adjusted for the current-frontier basis + query_split_adjusted_as_of for the point-in-time basis, BOTH coverage-enforcing; the as-of read caps the adjustment at the query end so a future split cannot leak in, and reports adjusted_through separately from the proven coverage_through). Verification: the crate's own L1 unit suite proves the math (forward/reverse/multi-split composition, the effective-date boundary, round-half-to-even ties, the symbol-only invariant -- a split adjusts only its own symbol -- non-OHLCV pass-through, non-equity + non-positive + overflow fail-closed), PLUS a generative property test (property_split_adjustment_invariants) that drives thousands of seeded bar + split sequences and checks identity / symbol isolation / compose-then-divide equivalence / order-independence / non-positive rejection (money math checked over generated inputs, not just fixed examples); the L3 contract test + tools/normalization_modes_check.py pin the split kind / Rust math / not-publicly-exported / CLI fail-closed / binding fail-closed invariants with per-guard non-vacuity mutations and a cargo round-trip (run the unit math suite, then assert data007_query_cli --normalization split-adjusted FAILS closed while raw returns stored values). SRS-DATA-012 STAYS passes:false: this is the HISTORICAL split-adjusted slice only -- the acceptance also names the LIVE subscription normalization path (the Market Data Subscription Manager is unbuilt) and the FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN modes (need dividend data + a reinvestment-treatment decision, deferred). DEFERRED (named owners): fully-adjusted + total-return modes and live-subscription normalization (SRS-DATA-012 remainder); scheduled corporate-action ingestion from a real provider (SRS-DATA-011; the fixture split batch stands in); an authoritative SDK<->core money-unit scale (the binding assumes the cents x100 fixture convention).

The data007_query_cli operator surface serves RAW and SPLIT_ADJUSTED -- the latter ONLY through the SRS-DATA-011 coverage-enforcing gate (MarketDataStore::query_split_adjusted), which fails closed when the symbol is not covered through the query end, so it never emits raw-as-adjusted. The StoreBackedHistoricalData strategy binding now serves RAW and the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED (binding_request_modes): it routes split-adjusted through the same gate and validates the echoed coverage_through frontier (gate-integrity), raising CoverageNotProvenError (naming SRS-DATA-011) when uncovered, never raw-as-adjusted. the named backtest / factor / notebook consumers now read via this path (SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE). core_library_modes are the modes the Rust core math implements -- RAW + SPLIT_ADJUSTED (the raw split math stays crate-internal; the coverage gate is the only public path to it). FULLY_ADJUSTED / TOTAL_RETURN remain in deferred_public_modes (dividend data, SRS-DATA-012).
