coverage_manifest_contract.description: SRS-DATA-011 CORPORATE-ACTION COVERAGE keystone (SyRS SYS-28a / StRS SN-1.14; BG-2 / BG-7). Acceptance: "Splits, reverse splits, dividends, delistings, mergers, and symbol changes are reflected in historical records so that backtests spanning corporate-action dates produce correct P&L calculations under the selected normalization mode." This ships the COVERAGE-MANIFEST half as FOUNDATIONAL substrate: a per-symbol completeness-through-date frontier, plus the SINGLE coverage-enforcing gate that finally lets the existing (S71) crate-internal split-adjustment math be served on the public operator surface -- but ONLY when coverage proves every relevant split is known. SRS-DATA-011 STAYS passes:false: only SPLITS / reverse-splits have adjustment math + coverage here; dividends, delistings, mergers, and symbol changes have NO math and NO coverage this session, and real provider corporate-action ingestion (Databento/IB) is deferred (the operator-set / fixture coverage frontier stands in, exactly as the SRS-DATA-011 verification step permits 'fixture market data, provider mocks'). The GATE (the crux an adversarial reviewer scrutinizes): the split math re-quotes a bar at event_ts t by every split with effective_ts > t (STRICT); every in-window bar has t <= end_ts, so the deepest requirement is that ALL splits with effective_ts <= end_ts are known. Define the coverage frontier D = max(complete_through) over the symbol's CorporateActionCoverage records (None if absent). query_split_adjusted PASSES iff a coverage record exists AND D >= query.end_ts; otherwise it FAILS CLOSED with CoverageError::NotCovered { symbol, have_through: Option<i64> (= D or None), need_through: end_ts }. D >= end_ts (not ==) is correct: a known split in (end_ts, D] applies uniformly to every in-window bar (consistent), and a split exactly at end_ts leaves the end_ts bar unadjusted (strict boundary) while adjusting all earlier in-window bars -- coherent with the >= gate. The result is an honest 'as-of-D split-adjusted' series with no phantom split-drops inside the window (the SRS-DATA-011 'correct P&L for backtests spanning corporate-action dates' property); splits with effective_ts > D are unknown but strictly after every bar the backtest sees, so they cannot affect in-window P&L. Over-claiming 'current-adjusted' would be the fail-open. COVERAGE REPRESENTATION: a new vendor-neutral DatasetKind::CorporateActionCoverage (codec tag 5, min_schema_version 3) in the SAME idempotent/durable/deterministic MarketDataStore, keyed (symbol, event_ts = D) with a complete_through = D field. event_ts = D makes the monotonic, append-only frontier fall out of the existing natural-key idempotency for free: advancing the frontier is a NEW key (Inserted), re-asserting the same D is a byte-identical record (UnchangedDuplicate, no-op), and a ConflictingContent fail is structurally impossible (a different D is a different key). Because the coverage record asserts the trust decision the gate reads and MarketDataRecord::new is public, store validation (validate_record, shared by upsert AND restore) requires every CorporateActionCoverage record to carry EXACTLY one complete_through field equal to its key event_ts -- a forged frontier (a key event_ts the value field does not carry, or a wrong/missing/extra field) fails closed before it can enter the store, so the gate's frontier is trustworthy. SCHEMA_VERSION bumps 2->3 (MIN_SUPPORTED stays 1); serialize() writes the MINIMUM version for the contained kinds (a daily-only store stays v1, a split store v2, only a coverage-bearing store is v3), restore() accepts [1,3], and the forward-compat guard rejects a v1/v2 blob smuggling the v3-only coverage kind. SINGLE GATED PUBLIC ENTRY POINT (no uncovered capability on ANY surface): the split-adjustment module STAYS crate-internal (lib.rs keeps `mod normalization`, private; split_adjust_records / SplitEvent are NOT re-exported). A sibling `coverage` module (crate-internal call into normalization) exposes ONE public method MarketDataStore::query_split_adjusted -> Result<SplitAdjustedResult, CoverageError>, which (1) requires the query kind be DailyEquityBar|MinuteEquityBar (else UnsupportedQueryKind -- so split_adjust_record's UnsupportedKind path is unreachable at runtime and a split-adjusted SERIES is equity-only by construction), (2) checks D >= end_ts, (3) only then pulls the symbol's split records bounded to effective_ts <= D (the as-of-D contract: a split in (end_ts, D] legitimately adjusts in-window bars, but a split with effective_ts > D is EXCLUDED -- even if its record is present -- so the result is never adjusted past the advertised coverage_through, since coverage proves completeness only through D) + the kind-narrowed equity bars and applies the crate-internal math, returning the adjusted records PLUS coverage_through = D. So the ONLY public path to split-adjusted output is the coverage-enforcing method: there is no public path to raw-as-adjusted. SURFACES: data007_query_cli now ROUTES --normalization split-adjusted through query_split_adjusted (split-adjusted requires --kind daily-equity-bar|minute-equity-bar; on NotCovered/UnsupportedQueryKind it exits non-zero naming have/need + SRS-DATA-011; on success it echoes normalization:split-adjusted + a coverage_through:<D> line); fully-adjusted / total-return STILL fail closed (dividends deferred). A dedicated data011_coverage_cli is the SINGLE coverage write surface (assert-coverage --symbol --through under StoreLock; show-coverage read-only). Coverage is NOT provider market data, so the generic market-data ingestion path refuses it at every layer: fixture_batch emits NONE for the coverage kind, DataLayer::ingest_market_record fails closed (MarketIngestError::UnsupportedKind) on a coverage record (the decisive data-layer boundary), and data016_ingest_cli rejects --kind corporate-action-coverage at parse -- so no generic flow iterating dataset kinds (provider_ingestion_kinds excludes coverage) can mint a trusted frontier. The Python StoreBackedHistoricalData binding now serves the gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED Protocol default through this same query_split_adjusted gate (RAW verbatim + gated SPLIT_ADJUSTED; an uncovered split-adjusted read fails closed with CoverageNotProvenError naming SRS-DATA-011, never raw-as-adjusted, and the binding validates the echoed coverage_through frontier for gate-integrity) -- and the named backtest / factor / notebook consumers now read split-adjusted via this path (SRS-DATA-007 is COMPLETE; see unified_query_runtime_contract). SRS-DATA-011 itself STAYS passes:false for the dividend / delisting / merger / symbol-change corporate actions. Verification: the crate's own coverage.rs unit suite proves the gate (pass at D == end_ts and D > end_ts; fail at D == end_ts-1 and at no record; split exactly at end_ts; split in (end,D] applied; frontier = max over multiple records; advance = Inserted / re-assert = UnchangedDuplicate / never ConflictingContent; non-equity query kind -> UnsupportedQueryKind) plus store.rs v3 schema tests; tools/coverage_manifest_check.py pins the coverage kind / gate condition / kind-narrowed gate / single-public-entry (not-publicly-exported) / CLI routing / coverage CLI invariants with per-guard non-vacuity mutations and a cargo round-trip that ingests a bar + split + coverage and asserts split-adjusted returns the ADJUSTED bar (close 2500 / volume 400000 / coverage_through:200) when covered and FAILS CLOSED naming SRS-DATA-011 when the query end exceeds the frontier. DEFERRED (named owners): dividend / delisting / merger / symbol-change adjustment math + their coverage (SRS-DATA-011 remainder); fully-adjusted + total-return + live-subscription normalization (SRS-DATA-012 remainder); real provider corporate-action ingestion (SRS-DATA-001/003/006; the operator-set / fixture frontier stands in); the named backtest/factor/notebook consumers now read split-adjusted via the gated StoreBackedHistoricalData binding (the SRS-DATA-007 consumer wiring this gate fed is COMPLETE -- StoreBarSource backtest, store_inputs factor loaders, and the binding all read gated split-adjusted).
coverage_manifest_contract.gate_condition: coverage_frontier(symbol) is Some(D) and D >= query.end_ts, else NotCovered{have_through,need_through:end_ts}

public_split_adjusted_reads.query_split_adjusted: current-frontier basis: adjusts through the proven coverage frontier D (adjusted_through == coverage_through). The basis a LIVE strategy wants.
public_split_adjusted_reads.query_split_adjusted_as_of: point-in-time basis: adjusts ONLY through query.end_ts (the as-of date), so a split effective after the as-of date is NOT applied (no lookahead). Reports adjusted_through == query.end_ts <= coverage_through; the factor-job store reader (the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 store read; the SRS-FAC-001 scheduled run) uses this read.
public_split_adjusted_reads.invariant: BOTH reads are inside the public coverage gate module and BOTH enforce coverage (D >= query.end_ts, else NotCovered); the split math stays crate-internal, so neither can serve raw-as-adjusted. SplitAdjustedResult separates the proven coverage_through (frontier D) from adjusted_through (the actual adjustment basis) so a consumer is never misled about the basis the bars are quoted on.

deferred: Dividend / delisting / merger / symbol-change corporate-action adjustment math and their coverage -- only splits / reverse-splits are handled this session (owner: SRS-DATA-011 remainder)
Fully-adjusted (splits + dividends) and total-return normalization modes, plus the LIVE subscription normalization path (owner: SRS-DATA-012 remainder)
Real provider corporate-action ingestion from Databento / IB -- the operator-set / fixture coverage frontier stands in (owner: SRS-DATA-001/003/006)

The split-adjusted series this gate serves is now consumed by the COMPLETE SRS-DATA-007 unified interface (the StoreBarSource backtest source, the StoreBackedHistoricalData strategy/notebook binding, and the factor store_inputs loaders all read it); SRS-DATA-011 itself stays passes:false for the dividend / delisting / merger / symbol-change corporate-action math + coverage, which this slice does not yet provide

factor_analysis_contract.description: SRS-BT-006 (produce factor analysis and tear-sheet outputs; SyRS SYS-18; StRS SN-1.05). The deterministic, dependency-free factor-analysis surface lives in crates/atp-factor-pipeline (module `factor_analysis`). compute_tear_sheet consumes a FactorPanel -- a per-rebalance-period panel of (SecurityKey, factor value, forward return) observations plus a quantile count -- and returns one FactorTearSheet bundling the three deliverables SYS-18 names: (1) InformationCoefficient, the per-period Spearman rank correlation between factor value and forward return (average tie ranks; Pearson of ranks; clamped to its [-1,1] domain) with mean / std / risk-adjusted IC; (2) FactorReturns, the per-period quantile-sorted mean returns (each bucket mean None when its bounding cutoffs are tied, so a constant/tied factor cannot expose an identity-driven ladder) plus the top-minus-bottom long-short spread with its mean (which assumes a REGULAR panel -- a spread scales with the forward-return horizon, so the average is the producer's contract; a compounded cumulative spread is deferred -- the panel cannot validate non-overlapping forward windows); (3) TurnoverAnalysis, the per-period top/bottom-quantile TARGET turnover (factor-signal; realized drift turnover deferred to the backtest engine) with means. Factor values and returns are dimensionless f64 (the factor domain, not a money leak); the work is deterministic (fixed left-to-right folds; cross-sections sorted by the total order (factor_value, SecurityKey)); an undefined statistic is None (never a fabricated zero); a non-finite aggregate fails closed; and FactorPanel::validate rejects an empty or degenerate panel at the trust boundary. The OPERATOR rendering surface is realized via the factor_tear_sheet_cli binary (see tear_sheet_cli): `defaults` proves a default run surfaces all three deliverables, and `run` renders the IC / factor returns / turnover of a fixture panel through the real engine, with `--quantiles` re-bucketing the SAME fixture and every undefined statistic rendered as the literal `undefined` (never a fabricated 0) -- so SRS-BT-006 is passes:true. Still deferred (owned by other features, not SRS-BT-006): the scheduled full-universe factor job that produces the panel (SRS-FAC-001), wiring real factor values/returns from the unified historical data interface (SRS-DATA-007), the REST/dashboard rendering half (SRS-UI / SRS-API), and bundling the SRS-BT-004 PerformanceMetrics family into one cross-crate report.

separation.comment: A bucket is factor-clean iff BOTH its bounding cutoffs are untied (cutoff_clean over per-bucket min/max) -- only then is its composition factor-determined, not a SecurityKey tiebreak. Per-bucket cleanliness is tracked SEPARATELY (bottom_clean / top_clean): the spread needs BOTH (top minus bottom) so it is gated on (true, Some(bottom), Some(top)); each turnover series is gated only on ITS OWN bucket clean at both endpoints, so a tie at one extreme withholds only that extreme's turnover, not the other's. A constant or cutoff-tied factor therefore cannot fabricate alpha, and an unrelated tie does not suppress an unambiguous turnover.

tear_sheet_cli.note: The CLI half of the SYS-18 operator tear-sheet rendering surface: `defaults` prints the CLI's published analysis parameters (DEFAULT_QUANTILES quintiles, the fixture universe / period count, the rebalance step) and proves a default run surfaces all three SRS-BT-006 deliverables (srs-bt-006-deliverables: ic=true returns=true turnover=true) -- a deliverable-AVAILABILITY proof, since there is no SyRS numeric quantile constant to match; `run` builds a deterministic fixture FactorPanel and runs it through the REAL compute_tear_sheet engine, printing the per-period + aggregate information coefficient, the per-quantile factor returns + long-short spread, and the per-quantile turnover. `--quantiles` is the override seam (FactorPanel::new(factor_periods, quantiles)): it re-buckets the SAME fixture (same securities / factor values / forward returns), so an operator re-analyzes a completed run without changing the data. The safety core (the SRS-BT-006 analog of the SRS-BT-002 'no cash fabricated' rule): every statistic the engine leaves undefined (None) renders as the literal `undefined`, never a fabricated 0 or NaN; a constant-factor (--pattern flat) run withholds EVERY statistic as undefined rather than fabricating a SecurityKey-ordering ladder; and a degenerate / non-finite / duplicate / too-small panel (--inject) or an invalid --quantiles 1 is rejected by FactorPanel::validate inside the engine before any statistic is printed (no partial sheet). The srs_bt_006_tear_sheet_cli integration test drives the binary in fresh processes. Deferred FOR THIS CLI (which renders a fixture panel): the REST + dashboard rendering half (SRS-UI / SRS-API), and wiring this tear-sheet to consume the scheduled full-universe factor job's real output (the SRS-FAC-001 producer is its own feature) over real factor/return data (SRS-DATA-007).

deferred: the scheduled full-universe factor job that produces the panel (SRS-FAC-001)
wiring real factor values and forward returns from the unified historical data interface (SRS-DATA-007)

rendering the factor tear-sheet to an operator: the CLI half is now REALIZED (the factor_tear_sheet_cli binary runs a fixture panel through the real engine and renders the three deliverables; see tear_sheet_cli). The remaining deferred half is the REST endpoint + dashboard view that render a completed run's tear-sheet to a web operator (SRS-UI / SRS-API), owned by those features, not SRS-BT-006
bundling the SRS-BT-004 PerformanceMetrics family into one cross-crate tear-sheet report

a validated COMPOUNDED cumulative spread -- needs each period's forward-return horizon to compound only contiguous non-overlapping windows (FactorPeriod carries a start timestamp only); only the per-period spread and its mean (the latter assuming a regular, consistent-horizon panel -- the producer's contract) are reported here (owner: the deferred SRS-FAC-001 horizon-aware producer, reading via the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 interface)

realized return-driven DRIFT turnover -- the turnover here is the factor-signal (target-portfolio) turnover; the held-position drift component needs holding-period returns and a rebalancing convention, i.e. the deferred backtest engine

validating PANEL REGULARITY (a consistent forward-return horizon + rebalance interval) before emitting the aggregate means (mean_spread / mean turnover) -- a spread scales with horizon and turnover with interval, so a mixed-horizon/interval panel yields means that mix incomparable magnitudes; FactorPeriod carries a start timestamp only, so regularity is the producer's contract, not validatable here (owner: the deferred SRS-FAC-001 horizon-aware producer, reading via the now-complete SRS-DATA-007 interface). The per-period series and the rank-based IC family are unaffected

factor_job_contract.description: SRS-FAC-001 (compute scheduled factors across the full US equity universe; SyRS SYS-32, SYS-33, SYS-51, NFR-P7; StRS SN-2.06, BG-3). The deterministic, dependency-free scheduled-factor-job surface lives in crates/atp-factor-pipeline (module `factor_job`) -- the upstream PRODUCER of the SRS-BT-006 factor panel the `factor_analysis` tear-sheet consumes (the panel-regularity owner that module explicitly defers to SRS-FAC-001). run_factor_job resolves its schedule against a TradingCalendar (the SyRS SYS-51 reuse boundary: the same calendar contract strategy scheduling resolves against -- is_session / session_open / session_close / is_early_close / next_session); it resolves the before-open start and deadline offsets against the session_open instant, so a session with no resolvable open fails closed (NotASession) and a lead that precedes the day start fails closed (ScheduleBeforeDayStart), enforces the HARD SYS-32/33 full-universe floor (the constant FULL_UNIVERSE_MIN = 8000, NOT a caller config, so coverage cannot be weakened from outside; a smaller universe fails closed with UniverseBelowMinimum), computes a user-defined FactorModel over each security's BOTH market AND fundamental inputs (a security missing either source, or for which the factor abstains, is an auditable SkippedSecurity with a FactorSkipReason, never a fabricated score), ranks the scored cross-section by the total order (factor_value desc, SecurityKey asc), and gates on the calendar-resolved deadline INSTANT (NFR-P7) read from an injected Clock: it reads the clock at the START (a run invoked BEFORE its scheduled start fails closed with StartedBeforeScheduledStart; one invoked AFTER the session-aware deadline -- even on a later session, which a bare minute-of-day would miss -- fails closed as a late start) and again AFTER scoring, ranking, and output construction (a run whose work crossed the deadline fails closed) -- FactorJobOutcome::DeadlineExceeded, no ranked set emitted -- so completion is gated against the ABSOLUTE session-aware deadline instant, and an early/late start or slow finalization is caught rather than assumed on-time. The gate is OBSERVATIONAL (start + completion): it catches every overrun it can observe -- including a slow-but-completing run, caught at the completion check -- but a deterministic dependency-free synchronous core cannot PREEMPT a hung FactorModel mid-call; supervised hard-deadline termination of a hung model is the deferred runtime (the orchestrator runs each job in its own resource-limited container, SYS-57; the NFR-P7 wall-clock performance test verifies it). Both the ranked scores AND the skipped list are sorted by SecurityKey, so the whole outcome is a pure function of the input set, independent of input order. The Clock is the injected timing authority for the run (production supplies a wall-clock-backed clock in the runtime -- the deferred owner of the live NFR-P7 wall-clock test; tests supply a deterministic clock so the run stays reproducible). Every security must be an equity (NonEquitySecurity; the factor universe is US equities -- binding to a trusted session-versioned manifest is the deferred SRS-DATA-001 catalog), the deadline minute is EXCLUSIVE (a run still executing during it is late), and a coverage ratio outside [0,1] (or non-finite) fails closed (InvalidCoverageRatio), and a run that SCORES fewer than the GREATER of FactorJobConfig.min_scored_ratio and the HARD platform MIN_SCORED_COVERAGE_RATIO (so a config of 0.0 cannot collapse the floor to one security) of the universe fails closed with NoUsableCoverage, so a near-empty result is never reported as a successful full-universe computation. The clock must be monotonic non-decreasing across the start and completion reads; a backward clock fails closed (NonMonotonicClock) rather than letting a late completion read as on-time. assemble_regular_panel additionally VERIFIES each period’s forward_window_end against the declared horizon through the calendar (ForwardWindowMismatch), so the horizon is verified rather than caller-asserted. assemble_regular_panel is the producer bridge to SRS-BT-006: it builds a REGULAR FactorPanel -- a constant calendar-resolved rebalance interval (trading_session_gap) plus a non-overlapping forward horizon -- exactly the regularity the tear-sheet's mean_spread / mean turnover aggregates assume but cannot validate (a FactorPeriod carries a start timestamp only); the assembled panel is re-validated at the FactorPanel trust boundary. Factor scores are dimensionless f64 (the factor domain, not a money leak); the work is deterministic for a PURE model (the FactorModel is invoked in canonical SecurityKey order, the cross-section is ranked by the total order, the deadline is read from the injected clock -- no clock of its own -- and there is no parallelism / RNG), so identical inputs yield identical output regardless of input order; a model with interior state spanning runs is the caller's contract to keep pure. The store-backed READ path atp_factor_pipeline::store_inputs::run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store sources BOTH the market (load_daily_market_input) and fundamental (load_fundamental_input) inputs for the universe from the unified historical store (SRS-DATA-007) by symbol / date range / resolution with no provider named (point-in-time SAFE loaders), assembles the SecurityFactorInputs cross-section, and feeds the scored core -- so a full-universe (8,000+) factor job runs over store-resident market + fundamental data, resolves its schedule through the calendar, and completes within the injected-clock deadline. Its data as-of is DERIVED from the calendar's session_as_of_ts(schedule.session) (not a caller timestamp), so a caller cannot pair a session with a future as-of (no lookahead); only the concrete real-calendar mapping is deferred (see DEFERRED below). The Rust integration test srs_fac_001_store_backed_job ingests 8,000+ securities' market + fundamental fixtures, runs the store-backed job, and asserts WithinDeadline with a fully-ranked cross-section (and that ghost securities with no store data are auditable skips, never fabricated; and that a malformed store record fails the assembly closed). SRS-FAC-001 STAYS passes:false (foundational store-backed wiring): the deadline in the fixture run is read from a DETERMINISTIC clock, which proves the deadline-GATING LOGIC, not completion before a real wall-clock deadline -- and the AC's verification mode is a PERFORMANCE TEST (NFR-P7), so the live wall-clock harness over real securities is the LOAD-BEARING deferred close blocker. DEFERRED: the live WALL-CLOCK NFR-P7 performance harness over real securities against a real clock (this slice gates against an injected deterministic clock so the late/early decision is falsifiable in-process; the real wall-clock clock is the runtime owner -- the close blocker); the REAL Databento/Sharadar network adapters that materialize the store records (SRS-DATA-001/005; fixture sources stand in); the CONCRETE US-equity calendar that provides the real SessionOrdinal <-> epoch-second mapping -- run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store DERIVES the point-in-time as_of_ts from the calendar's session_as_of_ts(schedule.session) (NOT a caller timestamp), so a caller CANNOT pair a session with a future as_of and no market/fundamental data after the as-of is consumed (the fundamental availability gate + the as-of-capped split read exclude anything dated/filed later); the binding is structural via the TradingCalendar port, and only the concrete real-calendar session_as_of_ts mapping (the same boundary as the rest of the calendar port; test calendars stand in) is deferred; the SYS-57 workload-priority admission of the job (priority 5, the 2 GB host-memory safety margin -- owned by the orchestrator workload-priority slice); and the operator schedule/factor-score surface (SRS-UI / SRS-API).
deferred: the live wall-clock performance verification over 8,000+ real securities completing before the real scheduled deadline (NFR-P7 performance test) -- this slice gates the deadline against an injected session-aware Clock so the early/late-start and late-finalization decisions are falsifiable in-process; the real wall-clock Clock is the runtime owner

the REAL Databento/Sharadar network adapters that materialize the store records (SRS-DATA-001/005; fixture sources stand in) and the concrete US-equity TradingCalendar implementation (SRS-SDK-002 / SyRS SYS-50) -- the SRS-DATA-007 store READ is DONE: store_inputs (load_daily_market_input + load_fundamental_input + assemble_factor_inputs) sources both market and fundamental inputs from the unified historical interface as point-in-time read primitives, and run_scheduled_factor_job_over_store composes that read with the schedule gate, DERIVING the data as-of from the calendar's session_as_of_ts(schedule.session) so a caller cannot pair a session with a future as-of; only the concrete real-calendar session_as_of_ts mapping (part of that deferred TradingCalendar implementation) is outstanding

the SYS-57 workload-priority admission of the factor job (priority 5; refuse to start below the 2 GB host-memory safety margin) -- owned by the orchestrator workload-priority slice, not re-implemented here

supervised HARD-deadline termination/cancellation of a hung or pathologically-slow FactorModel -- the in-process deadline gate is OBSERVATIONAL (it fails closed on an overrun it can observe, including a slow-but-completing run at the completion check), but a synchronous dependency-free core cannot preempt a hung call; the supervised execution context (the orchestrator container per SYS-57) is the owner, validated by the NFR-P7 performance test

binding a successful run to a TRUSTED session-versioned US-equity universe manifest and a market/Sharadar SOURCE-PROVENANCE manifest -- the inputs are caller-supplied; this offline producer validates what it can structurally check (equity class, uniqueness, finite inputs, full-universe count, scored-coverage floor) but cannot attest the inputs are the real trusted universe/data, so a successful FactorScoreSet certifies a correct COMPUTATION over the inputs given, not their trustworthiness (owner: the SRS-DATA-001 universe catalog + SRS-DATA-007 unified historical interface)

distinguishing a DEGRADED or STALE calendar (an unavailable calendar service) from a legitimate non-session -- the TradingCalendar port mirrors the SRS-SDK-002 Python calendar value-returning surface (a None is "not a session", not a health signal); calendar/dependency health is surfaced by the readiness/connectivity gates (SRS-ARCH-005, ERR-2) and the concrete US-equity calendar service (NYSE/NASDAQ/CBOE, early closes, DST), the deferred owner
the operator schedule and factor-score surface (SRS-UI / SRS-API) -- viewing the ranked FactorScoreSet and configuring the schedule/deadline

backtest_determinism_contract.description: SRS-BT-010 (produce deterministic backtest results for identical inputs; SyRS SYS-62; StRS SN-1.02). The BacktestEngine in crates/atp-simulation (module `backtest`) is already deterministic by construction (stable sort_by_key replay + integer minor-unit money math); this slice adds the module `determinism`, which makes that guarantee FALSIFIABLE. digest_result / digest_run fold a BacktestResult (and, optionally, the SRS-BT-004 PerformanceMetrics family) into a stable RunDigest via the same dependency-free FNV-1a + length-prefixed text codec backtest_store uses: the trade log and equity curve are folded as exact i64 minor units (encode_result_body carries NO f64 / to_bits -- no float-formatting nondeterminism in the money path), and the dimensionless metric ratios are folded through their exact f64::to_bits payload (encode_metrics_body), a bit-identical comparison. runs_match / metrics_match localize the FIRST divergent artifact (a trade-log/equity index, final equity, bars processed, or a named metric -- metrics compared via to_bits so a +0.0/-0.0 or NaN-bit difference is caught), Two harnesses run the engine twice over identical inputs (building a fresh strategy each replay via the shared run_pair helper, since BacktestStrategy is &mut): verify_reproducible checks the trade log + equity curve (two of the three artifacts), and verify_reproducible_with_metrics additionally computes the PerformanceMetrics for BOTH runs and compares them (metrics_match), covering the metric clause -- 'repeated runs produce identical trade logs, equity curves, AND metrics'. The metrics harness computes the metric family with the crate's own deterministic metrics::compute (the SRS-BT-004 family) over each run's equity curve + trade log against IMMUTABLE benchmark / benchmark_levels / config inputs -- there is no caller-supplied reduction, so no mutable state is shared between the metric computation and the backtest replays; a metric family that cannot be computed for a completed run fails closed (DeterminismError::MetricsComputation) rather than silently dropping the clause. That is deliberate: a caller closure sharing state with the run could either contaminate the second replay (computed between runs) or mask a run-induced change (computed after both); baking in the pure family removes that hazard entirely, so both replays receive byte-identical inputs and each metric set is a pure function of its result. A metric family that cannot be computed for a completed run fails closed via DeterminismError::MetricsComputation. runs_match also compares the result PROVENANCE (data_source and range, via DeterminismError::DataSource / DeterminismError::Range) so two results from different catalogs or date ranges cannot be reported identical merely because their trades and equity coincide. Both harnesses cross-check the canonical digests (DeterminismError::Digest) as defense for any field the structural compare does not yet cover. The work is deterministic (fixed left-to-right byte fold; no parallelism / random values / wall-clock read), so the verifier honors the property it checks. SCOPE: the module exposes the IN-PROCESS verifier (verify_reproducible[_with_metrics], two replays in one process) AND the cross-process closure. RunManifest + digest_manifest (ManifestDigest, magic ATP-BACKTEST-RUN-MANIFEST, `run-manifest:` Display) fingerprint the run's IMMUTABLE INPUTS -- code version, parameters, data source + symbol + a content digest of the input bars, date range, seed, starting cash, and cost model -- integer-exactly (encode_manifest_body carries no f64), so a verification PROVES the inputs were identical rather than assuming the strategy factory + BarSource are deterministic. The bt010_repro_cli operator binary (src/bin/bt010_repro_cli.rs) runs the fixture backtest in a FRESH process and prints the manifest + run digests, so the srs_bt_010_cross_process integration test runs it twice in two fresh processes and asserts byte-identical output -- closing the platform-generated-randomness clause that a same-process double-run cannot (process-seeded nondeterminism that is stable within a process but varies across a restart). The seed is recorded in the manifest for provenance, but the deterministic engine consumes no platform randomness, so a different seed changes the run-manifest digest while the run-digest is unchanged -- the 'platform-generated random values do not introduce nondeterminism' guarantee made observable. The operator surface is the local CLI binary, the same precedent as the SRS-BT-009 store CLI. Deferred: the REST POST /api/v1/backtests + dashboard rendering of this repeated-run workflow (SRS-API-001 / SRS-UI); the end-to-end guarantee under the real Python strategy host (the Rust<->Python boundary; binds strategy code version + seed; SRS-BT-001-runtime); and stamping the RunDigest onto each persisted SRS-BT-009 record (SRS-BT-009 store integration).
deferred: the REST POST /api/v1/backtests + dashboard rendering of this repeated-run workflow (SRS-API-001 / SRS-UI); the bt010_repro_cli binary is the local operator surface that runs the backtest twice in FRESH processes and closes the platform-generated-randomness clause, while the HTTP / dashboard surface remains deferred

the end-to-end determinism guarantee under the real Python strategy host (the Rust<->Python boundary) -- two identical runs producing identical results when user strategy code runs under the orchestrator, binding strategy code version + seed (SRS-BT-001-runtime)
stamping the RunDigest onto each persisted backtest record so a later re-run is proven reproducible by stored-digest comparison (SRS-BT-009 store integration)

Durable persistence of the ledger across a process restart (the NEW->...->terminal records surviving a crash) -- owner SRS-EXE-009 durable outbox / SRS-EXE-005 state recovery (SyRS SYS-90, NFR-R3). The ledger here is in-memory; 'stable across restarts' is guaranteed at the client-assigned key level, not yet at the store level.

Wiring the ledger into the real live (submit_live_order) and paper (accept_order) submission paths so every live/paper submission consults it -- owner SRS-EXE-001 / SRS-SIM-001 runtime + the Strategy Orchestrator. The pinned submission gates (error_handling / connectivity / freshness / live_designation contracts) are kept byte-stable; this slice ships the shared, source-neutral authority both paths will consult.
Driving transitions from real broker order events (ACK / fill / cancel / reject callbacks) -- owner SRS-EXE-006 IB adapter / SRS-SIM-002 fill model. SRS-EXE-008 stays passes:false until these land.

The finer-grained broker-rejection edges: PARTIALLY_FILLED -> REJECTED (whether a partially-executed order's remainder can be rejected) and the CANCEL_PENDING cancel-rejected-vs-order-rejected disambiguation (a rejected cancel returns the order to its working state; a rejected order is terminal -- opposite meanings). These are authoritatively defined by the IB adapter's event mapping (SRS-EXE-006 / SRS-SDK-004); only the unambiguous ACKED -> REJECTED post-ack rejection is modelled here, the rest is deferred to the adapter rather than guessed.

order_event_dispatch_contract.description: Source-neutral Rust order-event callback category authority for SRS-SDK-004. AGENTS.md forbids the Rust core runtime services from depending on the Python Strategy SDK, so the live (atp-execution, SRS-EXE-001) and paper (atp-simulation, SRS-SIM-001) dispatchers derive WHICH callback category to emit for WHICH OrderState transition from a single shared authority in atp-types (OrderEventCategory::for_transition); identical transitions therefore yield identical categories for live and paper by construction (SRS-SDK-001 / AC-14 parity), not by runtime coincidence. SCOPE: this contract covers the event CATEGORY derivation + the per-category field-presence REQUIREMENT only (the Rust analog of the Python SDK assert_order_event_payload rules). It models no money values (no f64 price/commission), performs no delivery, and measures no latency. The AC field VALUES, actual delivery to Python, and the NFR-P4 p95 latency PROOF require the real runtime dispatchers and are deferred (see deferred[]). SRS-SDK-004 stays passes:false in feature_list.json until those land; this Rust surface + tools/order_event_dispatch_check.py are the prerequisite the production dispatchers consume. Latency budgets are NOT duplicated here -- latency_source_block names strategy_api_order_events_contract as the single source of truth, and the check pins the Rust consts == that block's values == the Python SDK constants. SAFETY: a callback cannot be fabricated. OrderEventCategory is an OPAQUE newtype over a PRIVATE variant enum (private_variant_enum, Category) -- a dispatcher crate cannot construct one (unlike a bare #[non_exhaustive] enum, whose unit variants stay nameable) -- and the destination-state mapper (private_state_mapper, for_state) plus the deriver (crate_internal_deriver, for_transition) are non-public; the only public way to obtain a callback is the order-bound entry point (public_derivation_entry_point) OrderLedger::transition_with_event, which feeds the TRACKED order's real current state as `from` and returns a category only when the underlying mutation succeeds -- so 'no callback without an actual, serialized order-state mutation' holds by construction. transition_with_event returns ALL events a transition produces as a Vec<event_struct, OrderEvent>: the transitioned order PLUS any cascaded auto-rejection of a held cancel-replace replacement (which is terminal afterward, so its REJECTED callback could never be re-derived -- omitting it would silently lose a strategy callback). The check enforces the visibilities, the opacity, and the cascade aggregation.
deferred: Live IB broker order events (ACK / fill / partial-fill / cancel / reject) -> OrderEventCategory::for_transition -> populate fill price/quantity/commission -> Strategy.on_order_event, plus the live <1000 ms p95 proof -- owner SRS-EXE-001 / SRS-EXE-006 IB adapter (needs a running IB Gateway).

Internal simulation engine simulated fills -> OrderEventCategory::for_transition -> populate fill economics -> Strategy.on_order_event, plus the paper <100 ms p95 proof -- owner SRS-SIM-001 / SRS-SIM-002 fill model (needs a running simulation engine).

The AC field VALUES themselves (fill_price, fill_quantity, commission as exact money) are produced by the broker (live) or fill model (paper) at runtime; this slice fixes only the category and which fields must be present. The Python SDK OrderEvent payload + assert_order_event_payload (strategy_api_order_events_contract) own the Python-facing field-presence contract; this block is the Rust-core mirror of the same rules.

STATE-PRESERVING events are NOT representable by this transition-derived authority and are explicitly out of scope. for_transition derives the callback from the lifecycle DESTINATION STATE, so it can only represent events that CHANGE state. A cancel rejection (the broker refuses a pending cancel; the order stays working with no state change) produces no transition to classify, and -- because OrderEventCategory is opaque -- an adapter cannot mint one either. Representing such events needs a SEPARATE source-neutral broker/simulator EVENT-KIND API (it is NOT an OrderEventCategory), owned by SRS-EXE-006 (IB adapter) / SRS-SIM-002 (sim fill model). Duplicate / stale / out-of-order broker events that map to no legal modeled transition fail closed here; the adapter dedups/reconciles them against the acknowledged broker id. SRS-SDK-004 stays passes:false precisely because this event-kind half is unbuilt; it is named and deferred rather than guessed.

Enforcing that runtime dispatchers cannot bypass callback production: OrderLedger::transition (the raw SRS-EXE-008 state mutation) is still public, so a future dispatcher could mutate an order into a callback-bearing state without emitting the OrderEvent. The live (SRS-EXE-001) / paper (SRS-SIM-001) dispatchers MUST drive lifecycle exclusively through OrderLedger::transition_with_event (the event-producing path). Restricting transition's visibility to crate-private to enforce this by the type system is an SRS-EXE-008 API change (it is in order_lifecycle_contract.ledger.methods and is called by 31 sites in the EXE-008 integration test) and is deferred to the dispatcher-wiring work so EXE-008's tests + contract migrate atomically; bundling it here would be an unrelated cross-feature refactor.

order_type_contract.description: SDK-surface contract for SRS-EXE-003 ('support market, limit, stop, and stop-limit orders for equities and options in live and paper modes'). SCOPE: the *source-neutral order-type vocabulary* + the shared *price-validation rule* only. The four order types, the buy/sell side vocabulary, the equity/option asset class, the price-requirement matrix (which types carry a limit and/or stop price), and the price-positivity rule live in crates/atp-types/src/order_type.rs (OrderType / OrderSide / OrderTypeError) plus the crate-root AssetClass. The point of putting them in the leaf crate is that BOTH execution paths can share ONE definition rather than two that drift -- a PREREQUISITE for the SRS-EXE-003 AC ('each order type ... accepted [and] validated ... in both live adapter test mode and internal simulation'). As of SRS-EXE-003 BOTH paths consume the definition: the paper path (atp-simulation, SRS-SIM-001) RE-EXPORTS it (pub use atp_types::AssetClass; pub use atp_types::order_type::OrderSide as Side; pub use atp_types::order_type::OrderType), and the live path now carries it too -- atp_types::OrderSubmission was enriched with asset_class / side / order_type (+ OrderSubmission::validate delegating to OrderType::validate_prices, the SAME rule the paper intake applies), and the IB brokerage adapter (InteractiveBrokersBrokerage::submit_order) VALIDATES each order type before submission, failing closed with AdapterError::InvalidOrder so a malformed order never reaches the broker. The live-adapter-test-mode evidence (each of the four EQUITY order types accepted+validated+acknowledged over a deterministic gateway; non-positive prices / blank symbols / non-positive quantities fail closed without reaching the gateway; and OPTION orders fail closed pending the contract-identity model) is crates/atp-adapters/tests/srs_exe_003_order_types.rs. SRS-EXE-003 STAYS passes:false because the AC's end-to-end proof needs the deferred halves below: the real-IB wire (operator-gated, SRS-EXE-006); the OrderSubmission -> PaperOrderRequest/OrderLeg enrichment that the real PaperSimulationEngine::accept_order consumes (SRS-ORCH-* / SRS-SIM-001 seam); and the OPTION contract-identity model (underlying + expiration + strike + right) now LANDS in SRS-EXE-004 (atp_types::OptionContractIdentity + the multi-leg CompositeOrderSubmission path; see composite_order_contract) -- what remains for THIS slice is wiring that identity into SINGLE-LEG live option submission (SRS-EXE-003 / SRS-DATA-004), until which single-leg option orders fail closed, mirroring SecurityKey's fail-closed-on-Option. These types were HOISTED out of atp-simulation's paper_order module (where they originated under SRS-SIM-001) so there is now exactly one definition. OrderType encodes its trigger/limit prices in the variants (a Limit cannot lack a limit price; a Market cannot carry one), so the contradictory-price-set bug class is unrepresentable BY CONSTRUCTION. Price POSITIVITY is a separate rule (OrderType::validate_prices, mirroring paper_order::validate_leg and sim.rs NonPositivePrice): because the variants are public it is NOT construction-enforced -- it is an INTAKE-boundary check BOTH intakes apply (paper_order::validate_leg; and, as of SRS-EXE-003, the live OrderSubmission::validate, called by the IB adapter + dispatch_order + submit_live_order before the broker port). Money is integer minor units (never f64). Identical to the SDK-004 / ERR-9 partial-pass pattern.

python_parity.note: The Python authoring surface (atp_strategy.api.OrderRequest) carries ergonomic floats (limit_price / stop_price); the source-neutral core authority carries integer minor units (limit_price_minor / stop_price_minor). The pinned cross-language parity is the ENUM WIRE STRINGS ONLY -- OrderType / OrderSide / AssetClass member values, checked one-for-one against the Rust authority. The price-requirement MATRIX is pinned between the Rust authority and this JSON contract; it is NOT enforced on the Python OrderRequest, which today has no validation and permits a missing required price or a stray extra price. Enforcing the matrix on OrderRequest (an assert_order_request helper + tests) is DEFERRED (see deferred[]). The float-vs-int representation is deliberately a different layer; the SDK->core normalization is deferred with the runtime intake.

paper_consumer.note: atp-simulation's paper order intake (SRS-SIM-001) RE-EXPORTS this authority and does NOT redefine the order-type vocabulary, so the OrderType / OrderSide / AssetClass definition is shared, not copied. validate_leg DELEGATES price positivity to OrderType::validate_prices (mapping OrderTypeError into OrderError), so the paper intake and the live intake (OrderSubmission::validate, as of SRS-EXE-003) apply the SAME rule -- semantic parity, not a copy that can drift. tools/order_type_check.py pins both the re-export and the delegation.

fill_model_consumer.note: The SRS-SIM-002 fill model re-checks a raw OrderType's prices before any fill decision (evaluate_fill accepts a raw OrderType, not just a routed leg). validate_order_type DELEGATES price positivity to OrderType::validate_prices (mapping OrderTypeError into FillModelError) so the fill path applies the SAME single rule as the paper intake and the live intake (OrderSubmission::validate, as of SRS-EXE-003) -- no independent copy that can drift. Pinned by tools/order_type_check.py.

deferred: The REAL-IB accept->ack round-trip + the NFR-P1 acknowledgement-latency proof for each of the four order types -- owner SRS-EXE-006 (needs a running IB Gateway; operator-gated). As of SRS-EXE-003 the IB adapter (InteractiveBrokersBrokerage::submit_order) already accepts + VALIDATES each order type (fail-closed on bad price/symbol/quantity) and acknowledges it over a deterministic gateway double (crates/atp-adapters/tests/srs_exe_003_order_types.rs); only the live-wire encoding + the latency proof are deferred.

Internal-simulation accept->ack + the actual fill of a validated order (price-cross triggering, fill probability, volume cap) -- owner SRS-SIM-001 / SRS-SIM-002 (paper_order::accept_order exists for intake; the fill model is SIM-002).

Order STATE-TRACKING of an accepted order (NEW -> PENDING_SUBMIT -> ACKED -> ...) is the SRS-EXE-008 OrderLifecycle / OrderState machine; this slice fixes the order-TYPE vocabulary + price validation only, not the lifecycle wiring.
Routing of all non-live strategy orders into the simulation engine (so paper orders never reach IB) -- owner SRS-EXE-002.

Option CONTRACT IDENTITY (underlying + expiration + strike + call/put right) + the multi-leg composite envelope now LAND in SRS-EXE-004 (atp_types::OptionContractIdentity + CompositeOrderSubmission + IB adapter submit_composite_order; see composite_order_contract). What remains deferred: wiring that identity into the SINGLE-LEG live OrderSubmission envelope (this SRS-EXE-003 slice keeps single-leg option orders fail-closed) -- owner SRS-EXE-003 / SRS-DATA-004; and the real IB combo WIRE (operator-gated SRS-EXE-006).

The Python AUTHORING->core wiring: OrderRequest (Python floats) -> OrderType / OrderLeg (core minor units) for the strategy-facing order API -- owner the Python Strategy API / SRS-SDK. The core live submission envelope OrderSubmission now carries asset_class / side / order_type (+ OrderSubmission::validate) as of SRS-EXE-003; what remains is the Python authoring layer that constructs and validates it before it reaches the core envelope.

Enforcing the price-requirement MATRIX on the Python OrderRequest authoring surface (an assert_order_request helper rejecting a LIMIT with no limit_price, a MARKET with a stray price, etc., mirroring OrderType::validate_prices). OrderRequest is a plain dataclass with no validation today; the matrix is enforced in the Rust core and pinned to this JSON contract, but the Python surface pins only the enum wire strings. Owner: the SRS-SDK runtime / strategy host that submits OrderRequests.

Making price positivity unbypassable BY CONSTRUCTION (validated constructors + private price fields, or a validated order-type wrapper required by the submission APIs) rather than an intake-boundary check. OrderType's variants are public, so a caller can construct a non-positive price and skip validate_prices; today positivity is enforced where orders are accepted (paper_order::validate_leg, which delegates to validate_prices). Privatizing the fields is an SRS-SIM-001 API change (it touches every OrderType::Limit { .. } match/construction site across atp-simulation) and is deferred to the order-intake wiring so SIM-001's tests + contract migrate atomically -- bundling it here would be an unrelated cross-feature refactor.

composite_order_contract.description: SDK-surface + adapter-seam contract for SRS-EXE-004 ('support multi-leg options orders as composite transactions'). SCOPE: the vendor-neutral option-contract-identity model + the multi-leg composite order envelope + fail-closed validation + the IB adapter composite-submit seam. atp_types::OptionContractIdentity (underlying + expiration + strike_minor + call/put right; private fields, fail-closed constructor -- blank underlying / non-positive strike / impossible calendar date rejected) is the identity BOTH OrderSubmission::validate and SecurityKey::new previously deferred to SRS-EXE-004 (keying an option by its underlying alone would conflate distinct contracts). atp_types::CompositeOrderSubmission carries a StrategyId + two-or-more CompositeOrderLegs; each leg carries an OptionContractIdentity (options-only BY CONSTRUCTION -- an equity leg is not representable) + side + quantity + order_type. CompositeOrderSubmission::validate fails closed on: fewer than two legs (SYS-4), any leg with a non-positive quantity, and any leg whose order type carries a non-positive trigger/limit price (delegating to OrderType::validate_prices, the SAME shared authority the single-leg and paper paths use, so live/paper cannot drift). One bad leg rejects the WHOLE composite (atomic -- nothing partial routes). The IB brokerage adapter exposes BrokerageAdapter::submit_composite_order over the IbGatewayConnection transport seam: it VALIDATES then submits the composite as ONE IB combo order returning ONE OrderReceipt (one broker order id, never one per leg); an invalid composite fails closed with AdapterError::InvalidOrder and NEVER reaches the gateway; the connectionless discovery handle returns NotConfigured (never fabricates a composite). CRITICALLY, a composite routes as one combo order so it must pass the SAME ERR-1/2/3 live safeguards as a single-leg order before the broker: ExecutionEngine::submit_live_composite_order (+ the route_composite_order authority gate) mirrors submit_live_order/route_order -- non-live mode, IB connectivity (ERR-2 / SRS-SAFE-003), and market-data freshness for EVERY leg's full OPTION CONTRACT identity (ERR-3 / SRS-MD-004 -- keyed by OptionContractIdentity::canonical_key, NOT the underlying, so distinct strikes/expiries/rights on one underlying are not conflated; a composite is atomic, so ANY stale contract blocks the whole order; the per-contract freshness IMPL is the deferred SRS-MD-001 / SRS-DATA-004 wiring) are checked, then CompositeOrderSubmission::validate immediately before the LiveCompositeBrokerageSubmit port, all in the same precedence order; failures surface as StructuredCompositeOrderError (parallel to StructuredOrderError, carrying the unchanged CompositeOrderSubmission, reusing the same SYS-64 OrderErrorCategory buckets). The live-adapter-test-mode evidence (a four-leg iron condor submits as one broker order over a deterministic gateway; empty / single-leg / bad-leg composites fail closed without reaching the gateway) is crates/atp-adapters/tests/srs_exe_004_composite_order.rs; the execution-layer gate evidence (non-live / connectivity / any-leg-stale / malformed / non-designated composites blocked before the broker port) is the atp-execution composite tests, pinned by tests/domain/test_composite_order.py. The PAPER 'one composite' half reuses the internal simulation engine (PaperOrderRequest::MultiLeg, SRS-SIM-001) -- crates/atp-simulation/tests/srs_exe_004_paper_composite.rs pins the four-leg case. The L7 safety pin is tests/domain/test_composite_order.py. SRS-EXE-004 STAYS passes:false because the AC's end-to-end proof needs the deferred halves below.

execution_gate.authority: submit_live_composite_order is pub(crate); the ONLY public live entry is route_composite_order, which derives live-ness from the engine-owned LiveDesignation (a self-asserted StrategyMode::Live cannot reach the broker). Stricter than single-leg submit_live_order, which stays pub because ERR-1/2/3 tests pin it.
execution_gate.note: Mirrors submit_live_order/route_order so a composite passes the SAME ERR-1/2/3 safeguards before the broker port; freshness is keyed by each leg's full OptionContractIdentity::canonical_key (NOT the underlying), so distinct contracts on one underlying are not conflated (atomic -> any stale contract blocks). Pinned by tests/domain/test_composite_order.py.

paper_consumer.note: The internal simulation engine already routes a multi-leg options order as ONE composite (OrderRouting::InternalSimulation { composite: true }, SRS-SIM-001). SRS-EXE-004 reuses it for the paper half; the CompositeOrderSubmission -> PaperOrderRequest::MultiLeg bridge that a live-authored composite would flow through is the same deferred orchestrator seam as the single-leg OrderSubmission -> OrderLeg bridge.

deferred: The REAL IB TWS combo/BAG order WIRE for a multi-leg composite -- owner SRS-EXE-006 (needs a running IB Gateway; operator-gated). The deterministic accept/validate/one-receipt path works today (srs_exe_004_composite_order.rs); TcpIbGateway::submit_composite_order fails closed with LIVE_WIRE_PROTOCOL_PENDING behind the non-default ib-live-transport feature until the operator IB paper-account integration test (SYS-2e) completes the wire.

The DASHBOARD composite-position display ('displayed as one composite dashboard position') -- owner SRS-UI-002 (the dashboard stack is not built). The identity + composite envelope carry everything a composite position needs (canonical_key groups the legs), but rendering it is the dashboard's concern.

Wiring OptionContractIdentity into the SINGLE-LEG live OrderSubmission envelope and the market-data SecurityKey subscription key -- owners SRS-EXE-003 / SRS-MD-001 / SRS-DATA-004. This feature delivers the identity TYPE they consume; it does NOT change their still-fail-closed-on-Option behavior.

The CompositeOrderSubmission -> PaperOrderRequest::MultiLeg sim-engine bridge (so a live-authored composite simulates through the same legs) -- the same deferred SRS-ORCH-* / SRS-SIM-001 seam as the single-leg OrderSubmission -> OrderLeg bridge.

The Python multi-leg AUTHORING surface (atp_strategy.api -- a strategy constructing a composite from ergonomic floats before it reaches the core envelope) -- owner the Python Strategy API / SRS-SDK runtime.

Order STATE-TRACKING of an accepted composite (one lifecycle for the whole combo) via the SRS-EXE-008 OrderLifecycle machine -- this slice fixes the composite envelope + validation + adapter seam only, not the lifecycle wiring.
The IB combo NET-limit price (a single net debit/credit for the spread rather than per-leg order types) -- a refinement of the combo wire, deferred with the operator-gated SRS-EXE-006 wire.

destination_decision.note: route_destination is NOT a new source of truth: it maps the engine-owned SRS-EXE-001 LiveRoutingDecision authority to a destination. Authorized -> LiveBrokerage; NotDesignated -> InternalSimulation. The NotDesignated (non-live) arm must yield InternalSimulation and must NEVER yield LiveBrokerage -- the AC-10 'paper orders never create IB orders' invariant, unrepresentable for a non-live strategy by construction.

dispatch_guard.note: AC-10 forbids a paper order from CREATING AN IB ORDER, so the InternalSimulation arm must not reach an IB-order-creating path: no self.route_order (which leads to the broker) and no broker submit_order. It deliberately does NOT forbid read-only gates: connectivity (.state) is IB-order-submission reachability that paper never needs, and freshness (.freshness) is the SHARED market-data staleness gate that SRS-MD-004 requires for SIMULATED order submission too (block simulated orders on stale data, SYS-87) -- forbidding .freshness here would codify a stale-data bypass and block MD-004, so it is intentionally allowed (the simulated-order stale gate itself is MD-004, deferred). The LiveBrokerage arm delegates to route_order (the SRS-EXE-001 authority gate + ERR-1/2/3 connectivity/freshness safeguards) and must not touch the simulation port. Method-call tokens are matched (leading dot) so the check is robust to the receiver being on a previous line after rustfmt.

dependency_direction.note: atp-execution must not depend on atp-simulation (SRS-ARCH-002 one-way dependency direction). The simulation destination is reached only through the InternalSimulationSubmit port, which the orchestrator wires to the real PaperSimulationEngine.

This is the SAME bypass already deferred on live_designation_contract: submit_live_order is kept pub because the ERR-1/2/3 contract pins it (tools/error_handling_check.py check_submit_live_order_signature; err_1/2/3 integration tests call it directly). STRATEGY-IDENTITY AUTHENTICATION (adversarial R6, structural boundary): dispatch_order derives the route from submission.strategy_id, but StrategyId is a caller-claimed public field of OrderSubmission, so identity is by-claim, not bound to the authenticated strategy runtime. This is PRE-EXISTING and identical in the EXE-001 path: ExecutionEngine::route_order at lib.rs:506 also routes on authority_for(&submission.strategy_id) -- EXE-002 inherits the trust model and does NOT worsen it. Binding identity to an authenticated StrategyRuntimeBoundary (each strategy runs in its own orchestrator-managed container; the boundary is the trust anchor) requires changing OrderSubmission + route_order + the orchestrator container->identity wiring -- a cross-cutting re-architecture of the pinned EXE-001 authority surface. PORT-TRUST / REAL-WIRING PROOF (adversarial R4, structural boundary): InternalSimulationSubmit is an abstraction; a misimplemented or broker-backed impl supplied to dispatch_order could create an IB side effect the panic-on-touch stubs cannot observe. The dispatcher's by-construction guarantee is only that a non-live order routes to the sim port and touches no IB port (proven). tiered_storage_contract.description: SRS-DATA-008 (SSD-primary / NAS-archival tiered storage; SyRS SYS-24 / SYS-67, AC-5, NFR-SC2; StRS C-5 / SN-1.26 / SN-1.27). Acceptance: 'All ingestion writes to SSD first; new data is synced to NAS; SSD retains at least 90 days of configured hot data; NAS is used for indefinite retention; storage growth estimates are documented in Section 12.1.' The tier COORDINATOR wraps the directory the SRS-DATA-016 MarketDataStore persists to (the substrate `store` explicitly deferred here): TieredStore (crates/atp-data, module `tiering`) owns an SSD-primary store directory and an NAS-archival store directory plus the write/sync/retention discipline that keeps them consistent. (1) SSD-FIRST: TieredStore::ingest upserts the batch into the SSD MarketDataStore and durably persists it (unique-scratch + fsync + atomic rename via save_to_path) BEFORE any NAS write -- so a crash or an unreachable NAS between the two can never leave a datum on NAS but not on SSD. (2) NAS SYNC (eventual superset): after the SSD write, push_to_nas pushes every SSD record missing from NAS via the idempotent upsert, so NAS converges to a SUPERSET of SSD; the push is over the whole SSD snapshot (not just the new batch), so a prior degraded ingest's records are caught up by the next successful sync without operator action. (3) >=90-DAY HOT RETENTION: the hot-retention window is configurable but FLOOR-ENFORCED at 90 days (MIN_HOT_RETENTION_DAYS) -- TierConfig::new rejects a smaller window fail-closed (HotRetentionBelowFloor), so 'at least 90 days' is a guaranteed invariant; the tier never archives a record inside the hot window off SSD, and retention_report independently verifies every hot datum known to either tier is present on SSD (hot_missing_from_ssd == 0). (4) NAS INDEFINITE: the tier exposes NO NAS delete; archive_cold removes a record only from SSD, and only a COLD record CONFIRMED byte-identical on NAS (nas.get(key) == Some(record)) -- so archival frees SSD without ever losing a datum (NAS keeps it forever) and never breaches the hot window; a cold record absent/different on NAS is retained on SSD (retained_unconfirmed), and an unreachable NAS archives nothing. NAS ACCESS TAXONOMY (the SRS-MD-006 hook): a single nas_access classifier decides Ready (reachable + distinct) / Unreachable (absent mount) / Aliased (a `.`/symlink resolving to SSD) for EVERY NAS path. On ingest the SSD write always commits first, then an Unreachable NAS yields NasSyncStatus::Degraded (a RECOVERABLE outage caught up by a later sync -- the primary write is never lost), while a reachable-but-broken NAS (corrupt/checksum-mismatched store, a conflicting record, lock contention, or an SSD alias) yields NasSyncStatus::Failed -- an INTEGRITY failure surfaced DISTINCTLY so it is never reported as Synced nor folded into Degraded (a recoverable outage). A REACHABLE-BUT-BROKEN NAS (an alias, a corrupt/checksum-mismatched store, a conflicting record, or lock contention) ALWAYS fails closed: ingest reports Failed, and sync_ssd_to_nas / retention_report / archive_cold all return TierError::Nas -- so an integrity failure (an alias in particular) can never let archival delete the only copy (same_directory rejects an alias at TierConfig::new AND in nas_access, catching even a post-construction symlink). An UNREACHABLE (offline-mount) NAS is the RECOVERABLE case, handled per the operation's purpose: ingest commits SSD then degrades the archival half (Degraded); the explicit sync_ssd_to_nas (whose purpose is to confirm archival) errors; retention_report reports nas_reachable=false; and archive_cold (whose purpose is to free SSD only when a record is safely on NAS) archives nothing (Ok with archived=0, nas_reachable=false, everything retained on SSD -- a benign no-op the eviction policy reads, not a failure). The hot/cold boundary is a pure function of (event_ts, now_ts, hot_retention_days) -- the caller supplies now_ts, so the tier reads no wall-clock and the classification is deterministic; all timestamps are i64 epoch seconds, no floating-point; no serde / external dependency (only std::fs + the zero-dependency store codec). Operator surface: the data008_tier_cli binary (ingest / report / archive-cold / sync over an --ssd / --nas dir pair, fixture-driven); the Rust integration test srs_data_008_tiered_storage drives ingest -> SSD-first -> NAS superset -> degraded-preserves-SSD-write -> recover -> hot/cold split -> data-loss-safe archival -> idempotent re-ingest, inspecting both persisted tier files. The Section 12.1 growth-estimate clause is documented in docs/SRS.md (inspection). SRS-DATA-008 builds + demonstrates the tier SUBSTRATE (the SSD/NAS tiering discipline, the retention verdicts, the no-data-loss alias guard, the Degraded/Failed taxonomy) via data008_tier_cli + the L7/L5 Rust tests + the L3 contract test over fixtures + persisted-file inspection, but STAYS passes:false: the AC's CROSS-CUTTING 'all ingestion writes to SSD first; new data is synced to NAS' clause is NOT demonstrated end to end, because a raw ingest path (data016_ingest_cli, the SRS-DATA-016 idempotency demo) still writes to a single store dir with no NAS sync, and the real provider adapters (SRS-DATA-001/003/005/006) are stubs. Per the feature's Step 4 ('leave passes false until the evidence proves the requirement end to end'), flipping passes:true needs the PRODUCTION ingestion paths routed through TieredStore so EVERY ingestion is SSD-first + NAS-synced. DEFERRED (named owners, none load-bearing for the tiering property): the REAL Databento/IB/Sharadar/option-chain network adapters that FEED the tier (SRS-DATA-001/003/005/006; the tier is provider-agnostic -- it stores whatever MarketDataRecords it is handed); transparent cold-read FAILOVER that serves an SSD-archived record back from NAS without consumer code changes (SRS-DATA-009); the eviction POLICY -- the 80% high-water-mark trigger, the inactivity recency window, never-evict live-strategy data (SRS-DATA-010; this module ships only the data-loss-safe archival PRIMITIVE the policy drives); real 1 TB SSD / 20 TB NAS CAPACITY + a real network NAS mount (NFR-SC2 deployment / SRS-ARCH-004 Docker Compose SSD/NAS bind mounts; growth estimates documented in SRS Section 12.1).

ssd_first_ordering.description: In TieredStore::ingest the SSD durable save must precede the NAS push, so the primary tier is committed before the archival tier is touched.

nas_sync_status.nas_unreachable_token: NAS archival directory missing or unreachable
nas_sync_status.nas_alias_token: NAS archival directory aliases the SSD tier

archive_safety.description: archive_cold drops a record from SSD only if it is cold AND confirmed byte-identical on NAS; otherwise it is retained on SSD (retained_unconfirmed). No NAS delete exists, so NAS retention is indefinite. ALIAS GUARD: because 'confirmed on NAS' is trivially true when NAS and SSD are the same directory, the tier rejects an SSD/NAS alias (a `.`/trailing-slash lexical alias or an existing symlink) at TierConfig::new AND re-checks it at archive_cold (where both tiers exist and can be canonicalized) via same_directory -- so a post-construction symlink alias cannot make archival delete the only copy.

deferred: ROUTE ALL PRODUCTION INGESTION THROUGH TieredStore (the AC's 'all ingestion writes to SSD first; new data is synced to NAS' clause). Today the tier is exercised by data008_tier_cli, but the SRS-DATA-016 idempotency demo data016_ingest_cli still persists to a single store dir via MarketDataStore::save_to_path with NO NAS sync, and the real provider adapters are stubs -- so an ingestion path can still write SSD-only. This is the load-bearing close for SRS-DATA-008 (owner: wire data016_ingest_cli + the SRS-DATA-001/003/005/006 adapters through TieredStore, or fold the raw ingest into the tier). SRS-DATA-008 stays passes:false until then.

DURABLE ARCHIVAL MANIFEST / CATALOG of expected NAS keys. retention_report proves NAS is a superset of the records currently RESIDENT on SSD (a necessary condition), but it cannot detect loss of ALREADY-ARCHIVED data: once archive_cold evicts a cold record from SSD, NAS is the sole copy and there is no manifest of what SHOULD be on NAS, so a NAS that later loses that record still reports nas_superset Satisfied. A full 'NAS indefinite retention' integrity proof needs a durable expected-keys manifest/catalog (owner: SRS-DATA-018 backup + validated recovery / a data catalog). Documented as the honest scope bound on nas_superset_verdict.

Real Databento / IB / Sharadar / option-chain NETWORK adapters that feed records into the tier (owner: SRS-DATA-001 / SRS-DATA-003 / SRS-DATA-005 / SRS-DATA-006; the tier is provider-agnostic -- fixture sources stand in, as the SRS-DATA-008 step permits)

Transparent cold-read FAILOVER that serves an SSD-archived record back from NAS without consumer code changes (owner: SRS-DATA-009; this slice provides the SSD=hot / NAS=all tier boundary the failover reads)

The eviction POLICY -- the 80% high-water-mark trigger, the inactivity recency window, never-evict live-strategy data (owner: SRS-DATA-010; this module ships the data-loss-safe archival PRIMITIVE archive_cold the policy drives)
